Job Description:

Department Overview

Join Our Business Intelligence and Insights (BII) Team within the Monitoring Coverage and Capabilities, where you will play a pivotal role in advancing our Financial Crime and Risk Management (FCRM) initiatives. Become a key player in our BII team, where you will help design robust data platforms, transform data into actionable insights, support critical investigations with analytics expertise, and drive innovation by developing cutting-edge tools and technologies. Make a real impact on how our organization leverages data to inform strategic decisions and accelerate growth.

Job Description Summary

Manage a diverse portfolio of business activities/ reporting. As the specialist this role is responsible to provide the design/ development of complex presentations, reports, dashboards and scorecards to support business needs; ensure alignment of metrics across the organization. Acts a technical working lead/ resource to others. Work closely with senior leadership on significant projects. Drive the streamline, healthiness, and simplification of the reporting stack, ensuring optimal efficiency and clarity.

Job Description

CUSTOMER
• Initiate and provide leadership, tactical / technical direction and planning input for all business relevant information.
• Elicit information requirements and articulate those requirements in a manner that can be easily understood by other data and business partners.
• Identify data elements to address reporting requirements.
• Streamline and improve existing reporting to eliminate errors and redundancies.
• Ad-hoc reporting and derived insight analysis as required to support internal and external business partners.
• Provide on-going communications on project status and systems capabilities.
• Lead on enterprise data visualization needs and ensure related activities to overall strategies are completed.
• As the leading subject matter expert, provide advisory or consultative services for supported colleagues and/or businesses regarding multi-format data visualization techniques.

SHAREHOLDER
• Adhere to enterprise frameworks or methodologies that relate to activities for our business area.
• Ensure respective programs/policies/practices are well managed, meets business needs, complies with internal and external requirements, and aligns with business priorities.
• Consistently exercise discretion in managing correspondence, information and all matters of confidentiality; escalate issues where appropriate.
• Ensure business operations are in compliance with applicable internal and external requirements (e.g. financial controls, segregation of duties, transaction approvals and physical control of assets)
• Participate in cross-functional / enterprise / initiatives as a subject matter expert helping to identify risk / provide guidance for complex situations.
• Conduct internal and external research projects; support the development/ delivery of presentations / communications to management or broader audience.
• Conduct meaningful analysis at the functional or enterprise level using results to draw conclusions, make recommendations, assess the effectiveness of programs/ policies/ practices.
• Monitor service, productivity and assess efficiency levels within own function and implement continuous process / performance improvements where opportunities exist.
• Lead / facilitate and/or implement actions / remediation plans to address performance / risk /governance issues.
• Actively manage relationships within and across various business lines, corporate and/or control functions and ensure alignment with enterprise and/or regulatory requirements.
• Maintain a culture of risk management and control, supported by effective processes in alignment with risk appetite.

EMPLOYEE / TEAM
• Participate fully as a member of the team, support a positive work environment that promotes service to the business, quality, innovation and teamwork and ensure timely communication of issues/ points of interest.
• Provide thought leadership and/ or industry knowledge for own area of expertise in own area and participate in knowledge transfer within the team and business unit.
• Keep current on emerging trends/ developments and grow knowledge of the business, related tools and techniques.
• Participate in personal performance management and development activities, including cross training within own team.
• Keep others informed and up to date about the status / progress of projects and / or all relevant or useful information related to day-to-day activities.
• Contribute to team development of skills and capabilities through mentorship of others, by sharing knowledge and experiences and leveraging best practices.
• Lead, motivate and develop relationships with internal and external business partners / stakeholders to develop productive working relationships.
• Contribute to a fair, positive and equitable environment that supports a diverse workforce.
• Act as a brand ambassador for your business area/function and the bank, both internally and/or externally.

BREADTH & DEPTH
• Work autonomously and accountable for acting as a lead within a specialized BI & Reporting function and may provide work direction to others.
• Provide seasoned specialized knowledge, advice, and/or guidance to various stakeholders and team members.
• Expert knowledge of reporting and BI platforms, tools and techniques used within enterprise and within broader industry and ability to effectively leverage it to support broad range of reporting and BI functions.
• Expert at utilizing data sources across the organization with ability to integrate data across multiple platforms.
• Expert at utilizing data visualization techniques / tools across the organization to support multiple businesses.
• Manages communication at senior and executive level.
• Work effectively across multiple business units with numerous stakeholders to deliver reporting and BI solutions.
• Scope of role may have enterprise impact.
• Generally, focus on broad range of complex issues that may span from medium – long term issues (e.g. 6-12 months)
• Undertake and complete a variety of complex projects and initiatives requiring specialist knowledge and/or the integration of cross functional processes within own area of expertise.
• Oversee and/or independently perform tasks from end to end.
• Generally, reports to a Senior Manager or executive role

EXPERIENCE AND / OR EDUCATION
• Undergraduate degree or advanced technical degree preferred (e.g., math, physics, engineering, finance or computer science) Graduate's degree preferred with either progressive project work experience or
• 8+ year of relevant experience; higher degree education and research tenure can be counted.

Must-Have Skills
• 5+ years of hands-on experience in SQL and Oracle development.
• Proficiency with Microsoft Azure (Rahona), Databricks, Python, and Power BI.
• Demonstrated ability to manage or lead Tier 1/2 projects, including leading a team of developers for 3-4 years.
• Excellent communication skills for presenting insights to stakeholders.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to interpret complex data sets Ability to automate manual processes using scripting or workflow tools.

Nice-to-Have Skills
• 2–4 years of experience working with Tableau.
• Comprehensive knowledge of Anti-Money Laundering (AML) processes. Proven experience with Case Management systems, like Oracle FCCM (Financial Crime and Compliance Management Cloud Service), supporting risk and investigative functions.

Who We Are:

TD is one of the world's leading global financial institutions and is the fifth largest bank in North America by branches/stores. Every day, we strive to make every interaction, product, and experience remarkably human and refreshingly simple for over 27 million households and businesses in Canada, the United States and around the world. More than 95,000 TD colleagues bring their skills, talent, and creativity to foster deeper relationships, ensure disciplined execution, and build a simpler, faster banking experience. TD is deeply committed to being a leader in client experience, that is why we believe that all colleagues, no matter where they work, are client facing. Together, we are reimagining what banking can be for our clients, colleagues and communities.

Our Total Rewards Package

Our Total Rewards package reflects the investments we make in our colleagues to help them and their families achieve their financial, physical, and mental well-being goals. Total Rewards at TD includes a base salary, variable compensation, and several other key plans such as health and well-being benefits, savings and retirement programs, paid time off, banking benefits and discounts, career development, and reward and recognition programs. Additional Information:

We’re delighted that you’re considering building a career with TD. Through regular development conversations, training programs, and a competitive benefits plan, we’re committed to providing the support our colleagues need to thrive both at work and at home.

Please be advised that this job opportunity is subject to provincial regulation for employment purposes. It is imperative to acknowledge that each province or territory within the jurisdiction of Canada may have its own set of regulations, requirements.

If you’re passionate about helping clients and building deep, lasting relationships, TD offers diverse career paths where you can grow your expertise and make a meaningful impact.

We're committed to your success and foster a respectful workplace where diverse perspectives are valued, everyone has fair opportunities to grow, and you can unlock your full potential to achieve your career goals. Here at TD, we hire and develop the best.

title: Sr. Analyst, Business Intelligence
job_description:
We’re building a relationship-oriented bank for the modern world. We need talented, passionate professionals who are dedicated to doing what’s right for our clients.

At CIBC, we embrace your strengths and your ambitions, so you are empowered at work. Our team members have what they need to make a meaningful impact and are truly valued for who they are and what they contribute.

To learn more about CIBC, please visit CIBC.com

What you'll be doing

As a member of CIBC’s team, the Sr. Analyst, Business Intelligence will be responsible for providing business intelligence and reporting support for all post-charge-off collections portfolios to facilitate sound business decisions, along with regular support for data mining, reporting, and strategic analytics. The role applies comprehensive knowledge and skills to analyze data in support of business intelligence and performance management. Responsibilities include managing KPIs, establishing targets and thresholds, and monitoring them on a daily, weekly, and monthly basis. The Sr. Analyst, Business Intelligence exercises independent judgment within defined procedures and documents accurate business requirements that clearly represent the client’s needs. Working with limited direction, this role independently manages all reporting and business intelligence processes, as well as ad hoc requests from partners, the business, and senior leadership.

At CIBC we enable the work environment most optimal for you to thrive in your role. Details on your work arrangement (proportion of on-site and remote work) will be discussed at the time of your interview.

How you'll succeed
• Business Effectiveness – Manage the full suite of recovery and Operations Reporting processes through dashboarding and KPI management, Manage target setting and performance management using scorecards. Identify and recommend changes, whether technological or for business optimization.
• Relationship - Management Manage relationships with all stakeholders, risk partners, and recovery collections managers. Provide daily, weekly, and monthly business intelligence on the recovery and operations portfolio. Deliver reporting performance for partner agencies. Build and maintain internal and external relationships to ensure proactive service for the leadership team and business partners.
• Communication - Demonstrate excellent verbal and written skills to effectively articulate ideas and opinions involving analysis and processes. Ability to produce process documents and report documentation that facilitate an easy handover process, while showing strong command of language, clarity of thought, and orderly presentation, is a distinct asset. .
• Reporting & Business Intelligence - The role involves not only managing delivery of reports but also providing business insights into portfolio performance. This includes tracking all KPIs and effectively managing the reporting and analysis of key performance metrics and timely delivery of ad-hoc requests and SLA’s .

Who you are
• You can demonstrate experience with. Demonstrate 2–5 years of experience in providing business intelligence and reporting through Power BI and Excel dashboards. Possess strong hands-on working knowledge of Excel& Excel Macros, Power BI, SAS & SQL. Knowledge of Power Apps or Power Automate is an asset.
• You are a problem solver. You have exceptional critical thinking, problem solving and decision making skills with demonstrated ability to independently identify and analyze business problems and recommend solutions.
• You demonstrate knowledge. Demonstrate a strong understanding of banking and credit risk functions. It is also beneficial to have knowledge of how products work and the profit and loss function.
• You have stellar communication skills. You have the ability to produce a variety of business documents Power point presentation for Leadership that demonstrate command of language, clarity of thought and orderliness of presentation. You also have the ability to express oneself and communicate with others verbally; recognizing that verbal communication is more than just language - it includes tone, style and structure.
• You act like an owner. You understand business and process requirements in order to define fairly complex processes or a sub set of processes affecting a line of business
• Values matter to you. You bring your real self to work and you live our values - trust, teamwork, and accountability

What CIBC Offers

At CIBC, your goals are a priority. We start with your strengths and ambitions as an employee and strive to create opportunities to tap into your potential. We aspire to give you a career, rather than just a paycheck.
• We work to recognize you in meaningful, personalized ways including a competitive salary, incentive pay, banking benefits, a benefits program*, defined benefit pension plan*, an employee share purchase plan, a vacation offering, wellbeing support, and MomentMakers, our social, points-based recognition program.
• Our spaces and technological toolkit will make it simple to bring together great minds to create innovative solutions that make a difference for our clients.
• We cultivate a culture where you can express your ambition through initiatives like Purpose Day; a paid day off dedicated for you to use to invest in your growth and development.
• Subject to plan and program terms and conditions

What you need to know
• CIBC is committed to creating an inclusive environment where all team members and clients feel like they belong. We seek applicants with a wide range of abilities and we provide an accessible candidate experience. If you need accommodation, please contact Mailbox.careers-carrieres@cibc.com
• CIBC is committed to clarity in our hiring process. All roles posted are opportunities we’re actively recruiting for, unless stated otherwise.
• You need to be legally eligible to work at the location(s) specified above and, where applicable, must have a valid work or study permit.
• We may ask you to complete an attribute-based assessment and other skills test (such as simulation, coding, French proficiency).
• We use artificial intelligence tools during the recruitment process. Our goal for the application process is to get to know more about you, all that you have to offer, and give you the opportunity to learn more about us.

title: Business Intelligence Manager
job_description:
We’re looking for a seasoned Business Intelligence Manager who is passionate about connecting data to business outcomes and crafting compelling stories that inspire action. The ideal candidate possesses a proven track record in analytics, excels at transforming complex data into executive-ready insights, and demonstrates a natural curiosity and collaborative spirit. You excel in highly collaborative environments, partnering closely with diverse stakeholders, while also valuing the autonomy to lead work end-to-end. You bring a trusted advisor approach, combining analytical rigor and influence, to deliver a partnership that is outstanding across teams.

Position Responsibilities:
Business Intelligence & Strategy
• Own the delivery of performance reporting and executive-ready insights.
• Translate business questions into measurable metrics and analysis, highlighting trends, drivers, risks, and opportunities.
• Lead structured analyses to find opportunities, operational improvements, and efficiency initiatives.
• Develop executive-ready recommendations, arguments, and strategic frameworks to inform critical decisions.

Performance Transformation & Operational Excellence
• Conduct end-to-end assessments of business processes to identify performance gaps and value-creation opportunities.
• Design and lead transformation initiatives that deliver measurable improvements in productivity, cost efficiency, and scalability.
• Establish performance management mechanisms to sustain results.

Leadership & Stakeholder Management
• Provide day-to-day leadership and coaching for analysts (or operate as a player-coach depending on team structure), setting clear expectations for quality, timelines, and accountability.
• Build strong relationships across stakeholders to manage intake, prioritize work, and ensure clear ownership.
• Communicate complex findings in a simple, compelling way for leaders and diverse audiences.

Required Qualifications:
• 5+ years of experience in business intelligence, management consulting, analytics, or related roles.
• Strong experience delivering end-to-end reporting and insights
• Self-starter and ability to independently lead analytics projects from ideation to impact.
• Strong storytelling skills with the ability to distill complex data into simple, compelling narratives.
• Demonstrates curiosity by actively exploring data to identify root causes and emerging opportunities
• Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.
• Exceptional problem-solving and analytical skills, including quantitative modeling and data synthesis.

Preferred Qualifications:
• Background in finance, accounting, or operations analytics.
• Familiarity with enterprise data environments and data governance guidelines.
• Experience in a large, matrixed organization (e.g., financial services or insurance) is an asset.

When you join our team:

title: Business Systems Analyst  (Data Modernization and Consolidation)
job_description:
Required Skills & Experience

- Bachelor’s degree in Business, Information Technology, Data Science, or a related field.

- 3+ years of experience as a Business Analyst on large‑scale data modernization, consolidation, or migration programs.

- Strong understanding of data management concepts, data platforms, and data governance principles including:

○ Data warehouse modernization

○ Data lake or lakehouse initiatives

○ Platform migration (on-prem to cloud, vendor to vendor)

- Experience gathering and documenting requirements across business and technical stakeholders.

- Ability to analyze data inventories, dependencies, and data quality issues.

- Hands‑on experience supporting data migration validation, reconciliation, and cutover activities.

- Strong analytical, problem‑solving, and organizational skills.

- Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills.

- Proficiency with MS Office (Excel, Word, PowerPoint).

Nice to Have Skills & Experience

- Experience in the Canadian banking sector or other regulated environments.

- Familiarity with reporting, dashboarding, or PMO tools.

- Exposure to data privacy, regulatory, or risk management requirements.

- Experience working with data architects or governance teams.

Job Description

Insight Global is looking for a Business Systems Analyst (Data Modernization and Consolidation) to join one of our largest banking clients on a 6-month contract. The successful candidate will be responsible for the following:

- Gather, analyze, and document business, functional, and technical requirements for a large‑scale data modernization and consolidation program.

- Work with business stakeholders, data architects, application owners, security/compliance teams, and vendors to define scope and readiness.

- Maintain inventories of legacy and target data platforms, data assets, and dependencies.

- Assess data quality, data dependencies, business criticality, privacy, and regulatory requirements.

- Support data migration, consolidation, and transformation planning, including wave sequencing and validation strategies.

- Develop and maintain data flow diagrams, process maps, data lineage, and target‑state documentation.

- Track program risks, issues, and dependencies; escalate as needed.

- Support data validation, reconciliation, and post‑migration verification.

- Prepare status reports, dashboards, and executive summaries.

- Support migration rehearsals, cutovers, and post‑migration stabilization.

- Ensure activities align with security, risk, and regulatory standards.

RATE: $55-65/hr

Business Intelligence Analyst / Developer II

Flight Centre Travel Group (FCTG) is one of the world’s largest travel retailers and corporate travel managers. The company, which is headquartered in Brisbane, Australia has company-owned leisure and corporate travel business in 23 countries, spanning Australia, New Zealand, the Americas, Europe, the United Kingdom, South Africa, the United Arab Emirates and Asia. FCTG also operates a global corporate travel management network, which extends to more than 90 countries through company-owned businesses and independent licensees. The company opened its first leisure travel shop in Sydney, Australia in 1982 and listed on the Australian Securities Exchange in 1995. Our purpose is to “open up the world for those who want to see”. Every day, we give people all around the world the opportunity to experience something really amazing – travel!

To learn more about Flight Centre Travel Group please click HERE

About The Opportunity

Are you a problem-solver with a passion for data analysis and visualization? We are seeking a Business Intelligence (BI) Developer/Analyst II to join our leading-edge team. In this role, you'll bring your expert Power BI development skills, SQL knowledge, and understanding of relational databases to bear, working directly with one of our most valued clients in the corporate travel industry.

You’ll be at the forefront of our analytics operations, translating complex datasets into accessible, impactful insights that can drive strategic decisions for our client. Your well-crafted BI dashboards and reports will become integral tools, contributing to the improvement of travel management processes and strategies.

Key Responsibilities
• Power BI Development: Design, develop, and optimize advanced BI dashboards and reports using Power BI. Ensure they are intuitive, efficient, and tailored to the client’s needs.
• Data Analysis: Conduct comprehensive analysis of complex data, identifying trends, and translating insights into actionable recommendations.
• SQL and Database Management: Leverage SQL skills to query, manipulate and manage data within relational databases. Ensure the efficient and effective use of data in support of strategic initiatives.
• Client Relationship Management: Work directly with our client, understanding their needs, and presenting your findings in a clear and persuasive manner.
• Help cultivate a safe, inclusive, and accessible work environment for team members of all backgrounds including people who are racialized, have disabilities (invisible or visible), require faith accommodations, and/or are LGBTQ2+.
• Do your part to encourage an inclusive and accessible work environment for all Flighties, regardless of age, gender, disability, ethnicity, faith, and LGBTQ+ identity.
• Participate in maintaining an inclusive and accessible work environment for all Flighties, regardless of their identities and lived experiences.
• Help to foster an inclusive and accessible team environment for all Flighties, regardless of their identities and cultural backgrounds.

Experience & Qualifications
• Bachelor's Degree in Information Systems, Computer Science, Business Administration, or a related field.
• Proven experience as a BI Developer or Data Scientist, with strong proficiency in Fabric & Power BI.
• Solid experience in SQL and working with relational databases.
• Excellent analytical skills with the ability to dissect large amounts of data.
• Strong communication skills and the ability to effectively convey complex information.
• Experience in the corporate travel industry is a plus.

title: Manager, Business Intelligence & Insights (18-Months Contract), GTB - Toronto, ON
job_description:
Requisition ID: 253795

Join a purpose driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

Global Transaction Banking

Global Transaction Banking (GTB) is the payments and transaction engine of Scotiabank. We support Small Business, Commercial and Corporate clients with effective treasury management solutions coupled with a best-in-class service model, making it easy for clients to do business with us.

As businesses build their digital capabilities and transform their operating models, their payment needs are evolving too. Boasting a unique global footprint, GTB’s comprehensive suite of innovative banking solutions help our business clients generate operational efficiencies, streamline and simplify payments, improve working capital performance, and mitigate financial risk.

At Scotiabank, we embrace your strengths, ideas, and ambitions. GTB is a fast-growing team with a focus on the Americas, particularly Canada, the U.S., Mexico, and we are seeking top tier talent to complement our organization.

Join a purpose-driven winning team, committed to results, in an inclusive and high-performing culture.

Purpose
The Manager, Business Intelligence & Insights for Global Banking Transaction Banking is responsible for leading the development and end-to-end delivery of business insights by partnering closely with business stakeholders, analytics engineering teams, and technology teams. The incumbent contributes to the overall success of GTB by transforming complex data into meaningful, actionable insights that support strategic decision-making in a timely and scalable manner.

The successful candidate brings strong technical, financial, and analytical expertise, along with the ability to communicate effectively with audiences up to and including Scotiabank executives. This individual is a dynamic and organized self-starter who thrives in environments of ambiguity, is highly detail-oriented, and operates effectively in a fast-paced setting. Strong leadership, business acumen, and an in-depth understanding of GTB products, services, and operations are essential.

What You’ll Do:
• Design visually impactful and intuitive dashboards supported by efficient and scalable data models, leveraging industry best practices and advanced modeling techniques.
• Demonstrate strong problem-solving and data storytelling capabilities through dashboards and visualizations.
• Deliver actionable insights to GTB business stakeholders across Sales, Product, Channels, Operations, Strategy, and Risk.
• Collaborate closely with Salesforce CRM teams to define enhancement requirements and resolve data quality issues.
• Support ad hoc analysis requests to inform and drive strategic business decisions.
• Ensure accuracy, governance, and appropriate access controls across the BI ecosystem.
• Identify, prioritize, analyze, remediate, and escalate data and reporting issues as required.
• Serve as a centre of excellence for GTB business, Product, and Payments analytics, acting as a trusted advisor on all matters related to data and analytics.
• Ideate and develop best-in-class automation solutions and reporting tools to support Sales, Payments, and Product teams in effective portfolio management.
• Partner closely with GTB Analytics Engineering and Advanced Analytics teams to solve complex business challenges.
• Build and maintain strong relationships with GTB business stakeholders across Sales, Product, Operations, Channels, and Strategy.
• Collaborate with GBM Client Insights & Analytics, Commercial Banking Analytics, and Business Banking Analytics teams to ensure alignment and knowledge sharing.
• Incorporate the Bank’s risk appetite and risk culture into day-to-day activities and decision-making.

What You'll Bring:

title: Senior Business Intelligence Engineer, Forecasting and Labs Analytics
job_description:
Description

Amazon has the world’s most complex supply chain: we fulfill global demand for hundreds of millions of products at lightning fast delivery speeds. We need your skills to optimize our supply chain, with the end goal of delighting our customers. A core part of the supply chain operations is Demand and Inbound Forecasting: we forecast the demand and arrival of tens of millions of products. The forecasts are used to make many decisions, such as automatically ordering hundreds of millions worth of inventory, deciding where to place that inventory, and establishing labor plans for worldwide warehouses.

We are looking for a Sr. Business Intelligence Engineer to join a team developing new tools and metrics to oversee the delivery of analytics solutions across the product development lifecycle, from entitlement through implementation and ongoing business operations. A successful candidate will be customer obsessed – the Forecasting org has 50+ unique customers. You will need to balance long‑term thinking with short‑term actions. You will be comfortable navigating an ambiguous environment, know how to confirm and reconfirm assumptions, and be able to harmonize disparate points of view.

Key Job Responsibilities

Analytics & Insights
• Lead projects using data modeling, and statistical analysis best practices to create reporting and give insight to the forecast against large, multi‑dimensional datasets from multiple sources
• Direct the design and implementation of analytics solutions for new product initiatives
• Ensure quality and consistency of analytical outputs across team deliverables
• Build tools to analyze large amounts of data that uncover forecasting issues and convey insights which highlight forecast performance and explain variance
• Oversee the development and maintenance of recurring reporting frameworks

Product Development Support
• Partner with product, science, and engineering teams throughout the development lifecycle to define success metrics and measurement approaches
• Guide analytics strategy during project entitlement phases

Business Partnership
• Translate complex analytical findings into actionable business recommendations
• Build and maintain relationships with cross‑functional stakeholders

A day in the life

Amazon offers a full range of benefits that support you and eligible family members, including domestic partners and their children. Benefits can vary by location, the number of regularly scheduled hours you work, length of employment, and job status such as seasonal or temporary employment. The benefits that generally apply to regular, full‑time employees include:
• Medical, Dental, and Vision Coverage
• Maternity and Parental Leave Options
• Paid Time Off (PTO)
• RRSP

About The Team

We are ASIN Forecasting Analytics Team. We monitor the ASIN demand and inbound inventory forecasts for Amazon businesses worldwide. We report on historical forecast performance, changes in future forecasts, and the health of future forecasts. We partner with stakeholder teams across supply chain, retail, and operations to assess the future forecast against existing supply conditions. Lastly, we develop intervention models to correct forecasts when defects exist or for scenarios that are not adequately addressed by core models. In addition to forecast accuracy, we help manage forecast volatility.

Basic Qualifications
• 5+ years of SQL experience
• 5+ years of professional or military experience
• Experience programming to extract, transform and clean large (multi‑TB) data sets
• Experience in scripting for automation (e.g. Python) and advanced SQL skills.
• Experience with AWS technologies
• Experience with theory and practice of information retrieval, data science, machine learning and data mining
• Experience with theory and practice of design of experiments and statistical analysis of results

Preferred Qualifications
• Experience working directly with business stakeholders to translate between data and business needs
• Experience managing, analyzing and communicating results to senior leadership

Amazon is an equal opportunity employer and does not discriminate on the basis of protected veteran status, disability, or other legally protected status. Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information.

Final compensation will be determined based on factors including experience, qualifications, and location. Amazon offers comprehensive benefits including health insurance (medical, dental, vision, prescription, basic life & AD&D insurance), Registered Retirement Savings Plan (RRSP), Deferred Profit Sharing Plan (DPSP), paid time off, and other resources to improve health and well‑being.

job_description:
Position: Business Intelligence Analyst

Reports to: National Director, Insights and Performance

Language Requirement: Bilingual (English and French) – Asset

Employment: Permanent, Full-time

Hiring Salary Range: $73,000 to $90,000, commensurate with experience

Location: Ottawa (preferred)

Posting Date: March 16, 2026

Reply Deadline: March 30, 2026

Opportunity

United Way Centraide (UWC) is Canada’s leading network of local community builders empowering people to make lasting social change in their communities. With a presence across Canada, serving over 5000 communities, our network of local United Ways and Centraides tackle the most important social issue of poverty and social exclusion. Our vision is that everyone in every community has the opportunity to reach their full potential. United Way Centraide Canada (UWCC) is the national office that provides leadership and services to UWC members across the country.

Profile

UWCC data and insight program is an integral part of a broader strategy to position the UWC network for long-term growth and impact.

The data and insights program is a strategic link between operations and business units with a mandate to advance UWCC’s strategic priorities including network wide strategic alignment, interoperability and performance.

Working with teams across the organization and reporting to the National Director of Insights and Performance, the Business Intelligence Analyst will contribute to the development of UWCC’s data insight strategy and maturity roadmap and will support their implementations in the following areas:
• Advanced business analytics – Proactive critical analysis of internal and external data sources, their utilization, and identification of opportunities for further insights to support business priorities and decisions.
• Empowered self-serve delivery models – Implementation of a “self-serve" principle into existing and new data insight products and services including capacity-building to support change management and enhance adoption.
• Data infrastructure, governance, and management - Enhancement of data flow processes, introduction and management of a data governance program.

The successful candidate for this position will have a solid understanding of data analysis, is comfortable working collaboratively in a dynamic and multi-stakeholder environment and has strong curiosity and passion to learn about emerging trends and needs in the community services sectors.

Responsibilities

ADVANCED BUSINESS ANALYTICS
• Work cross-functionally to identify business problems and requirements and collect, clean, manage, and analyze available and related data to advance UWCC’s work using meaningful insights
• Responding to data insight needs, analyze (mine) and interpret internal and secondary data sources, identify trends, patterns, and anomalies, providing insights based on analysis in easy-to-understand formats and reports.
• Organizing and presenting data to tell a cohesive story in support of leadership decisions and advancing organizational strategies through prioritizing key data sources, conducting analysis, and identifying key performance indicators.
• Provide on-demand analysis for integration into projects across business lines.

EMPOWERED SELF-SERVE DELIVERY MODELS
• Ensure business and end users engagement on current state analysis, requirements gathering and backlog prioritization, user acceptance testing, and training on all data insight products.
• Design, develop, disseminate, and maintain business intelligence solutions and dashboards to empower teams across the organization with accessible opportunities to explore data, uncover new insights, and take action.
• Provide leadership and support in design and execution of training and information sessions within UWCC and across the UWC network on relevant data, insight, and information management practices and products.

DATA INFRASTRUCTURE, GOVERNANCE & MANAGEMENT
• Work with IT and Operations team to improve data management capabilities through data mapping and modelling, identifying data anomalies, ensuring proper data hygiene, and keeping up to date on leading data management practices.

Qualifications
• Minimum 3 years’ experience in data analytics and reporting with working knowledge of business intelligence applications.
• Post-secondary degree in a relevant field – i.e., Business, Economics, Statistics, Mathematics or Computer Science.
• Competent in data analytics techniques – i.e., A/B testing, regression, trend analysis, projections.
• Intermediate understanding of programming languages such as Python, Perl, etc. with the view to processing datasets and automating data analysis, as well as experience with scripting and working with SQL databases. Programming skills in JavaScript / JQuery / PHP will be considered an asset.
• Advanced knowledge of Excel: data summarizing, statistical functions, optimization.
• Advanced knowledge of data visualization and comfortable working with data visualization specifically in PowerBI. Experience managing and working in Microsoft Fabric data environment with hands-on experience with data ingestion pipelines, data transformation and access management.
• Some understanding of DevOps principles as applied to version control and user access.
• Solid understanding of business intelligence with strong curiosity and eagerness to develop a deep understanding of the business, learn new skills, and technologies.
• Excellent communications skills, enjoys presenting data and complex analyses into stories understood by technical and non-technical stakeholders.
• Strong ability to work with non-technical business partners to gather requirements and plan out data products.
• Thrives in teams and can work under dynamic conditions and within deadlines.
• Experience working in philanthropy and/or community and social services is an asset.

Location

United Way Centraide’s national office is located in Ottawa, Ontario. The office is an open-space environment and at times, employees work from remote locations. We currently work in a hybrid-remote model. There is a strong preference for the successful candidate to be located in Ottawa but the opportunity is open to individuals across the country.

Diversity, Equity and Inclusion

United Way Centraide Canada hires based on merit and is strongly committed to diversity and equity within its community and to providing a welcoming and inclusive workplace. It especially welcomes applications from Black, Indigenous and People of Colour, women, persons with disabilities, people of all sexual orientations and genders, and others with the skills and knowledge to productively engage with diverse communities.

How to apply

Please send your cover letter along with your resume in confidence to: recruitement@E2Rsolutions.com

We thank everyone for applying; however, only qualified candidates will be contacted.

title: Data Operations Analyst
job_description:
• This role is open to remote based candidates based in Canada with a preference for PST*
We’re looking for a Data Operations Analyst to join our team and be part of our next stage of growth. As our Data Operations Analyst, your top priority is turning data into clear, actionable insights that help our Senior Leadership Team make informed decisions. You will work across Finance, Sales, and customer facing teams to deliver reliable reporting, analyze business performance, and surface trends that support operational strategic planning.

This role combines hands-on data analysis with cross-functional collaboration and a high level of ownership over operational reporting. You will be responsible for extracting, analyzing, and presenting data primarily from Salesforce, while identifying opportunities to improve reporting, data quality, and operational visibility across the organization.

Who we are
At Traction Rec, community is at the heart of everything we do. We empower nonprofit organizations like YMCAs, Jewish Community Centers (JCCs), Boys & Girls Clubs of America (BGCA), and Salvation Army Kroc Centers to strengthen their communities through great technology built on the Salesforce platform. By enabling these organizations to deliver their services more efficiently, we help them focus on creating meaningful connections and driving positive change where it matters most.

We believe that business is a powerful force for good. As part of this belief, we’ve committed to the Pledge 1% for the Planet, dedicating 1% of our time, product, profit, or equity to supporting environmental and community causes. This commitment reflects our dedication to creating a positive impact that extends beyond technology.

Our passion for community and innovation has earned us recognition, including:
• Salesforce Nonprofit Sales Partner of the Year and ISV Partner of the Year
• Globe & Mail's Top Growing Companies of 2024 and 2025
• Deloitte's Technology Fast 50 winner 2024
• Deloitte's Technology Fast 500 winner 2024 and 2025

At Traction Rec, we live our values: Community, Authenticity, Impact, Fun, and Growth. As a Living Wage Certified employer in British Columbia, we are dedicated to supporting not just the communities we serve but also the people who make it all happen—our team.

If you’re passionate about making a difference and want to join a company that puts community first, we’d love to have you on this journey with us. Together, let’s create stronger, healthier, and more connected communities.

Skills
Traction Rec’rs come from a variety of backgrounds, and we are eager to find people that are from diverse backgrounds. For this role, you might have:
• 3+ years of experience working with SQL, relational databases, and large datasets in a SaaS or technology environment
• Strong experience working with Salesforce data, including reporting, data extraction, and analysis using tools such as SOQL, Data Loader, or APIs
• Advanced Excel skills including Power Query, pivot tables, formulas, charts, and data analysis
• Experience building reports, dashboards, and visualizations using BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker
• Experience working with SaaS business metrics such as ARR, pipeline, churn, retention, and product adoption
• Experience leveraging AI-assisted tools to support data analysis, research, and reporting workflows
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to identify patterns, root causes, and opportunities for improvement
• Solid business acumen with the ability to connect operational, financial, and customer data to business outcomes
• Strong communication skills with the ability to translate complex data into clear insights for stakeholders
• Ability to work independently, manage multiple priorities, and collaborate across teams in a fast-paced environment
• Bonus points if you’ve worked in the nonprofit sector or with community-based organizations

What You Will Be Doing
• Serve as the primary point of contact for data and reporting requests across teams and the Senior Leadership Team.
• Own Salesforce reporting and data extraction for key KPIs, especially around usage and revenue.
• Pull, join, and analyze data from multiple sources to support reporting and decision-making.
• Build, maintain, and improve recurring and ad hoc reports using Salesforce and other analytics tools.
• Track and analyze key business metrics, including churn, customer lifecycle, and product adoption.
• Support Finance, Sales, Product, and customer-facing teams with data for planning, forecasting, and performance tracking.
• Prepare accurate and timely reporting that supports executive dashboards, quarterly business reviews, and board reporting.
• Identify opportunities to improve data quality, reporting processes, and operational visibility.
• Translate complex data into clear, actionable insights for stakeholders across the organization.

Compensation
At Traction Rec, we are committed to a fair, market-based and equitable compensation structure. Our market data is refreshed on an annual basis to reflect the most current market conditions.

The starting salary band for this role is $80,000-$90,000. Starting salaries will vary within this range based on experience, skill level, and internal equity related to the role.

Please note that the range details above reflect the base pay only and do not include our competitive bonus program and benefits that we offer.

Our Approach to AI
At Traction Rec, we’re big fans of AI and we embrace it as a tool that helps us work smarter and more creatively. It may even have helped with this job description. That said, our recruiting process is 100% human. From resume review to interviews to job offers, every step is led by real people who care about real people. No bots making hiring decisions here.

If you’re interested, so are we! Send your resume our way, and let’s chat.
At Traction Rec, we believe that diversity and inclusion are essential to our success. We are committed to building a team that reflects the diversity of the communities we serve, and we actively seek and welcome candidates from all backgrounds. Our hiring process is designed to be fair, welcoming, and respectful to all candidates, regardless of race, ethnicity, religion, gender, sexual orientation, age, ability, or any other characteristic protected by law. We believe that diversity brings unique perspectives and experiences that can enhance our team and drive innovation, and we are committed to providing equal opportunity and support to all candidates. We encourage everyone to bring their authentic selves to the interview process and join us in building a diverse and inclusive culture at Traction Rec.

Don’t meet every single requirement? Studies have shown that women and marginalized groups are less likely to apply to jobs unless they meet every single qualification. If you’re excited about this role, but your past experience doesn’t align perfectly with every qualification in the job description, we encourage you to apply anyway. You may be just the right candidate for this or other roles.

Please note that employment is contingent on the successful completion of background and reference checks.

title: Business Intelligence Data Analyst
job_description:
CAREER OPPORTUNITY

Business Intelligence Data Analyst

Location: Vancouver, BC

Status: Permanent, Full-Time

Affiliation: British Columbia General Employees’ Union (BCGEU)

Salary range: Job Class 23 (Market Adjusted)($81,542 - $93,249) *under review*

Competition #: B066-25

Application Deadline: April 10, 2026

VALUES STATEMENT:

Our commitment to the principles of fundamental justice embedded in the Charter of Rights and Freedoms, and to Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion is ambitious and foundational. It informs everything we do, as we defend liberty, equality, autonomy, and dignity; as we dismantle colonization, racism, sexism, ableism, transphobia and homophobia, and all discrimination. These are our values, and they inform our hiring practices too. Our business is aiding people in severe legal distress and necessitates internal and external alignment of practicing these values. Integrating Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion throughout every aspect of Legal Aid BC is critical to building that culture, and we prioritize ongoing resources to achieve that goal.

The Opportunity

As Business Intelligence Data Analyst reporting to the Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, this position is pivotal in transforming data into actionable insights through interactive and visual representations, your primary function includes defining and validating data elements and structures, developing subject area models, and maintaining data architecture across multiple systems. In addition, this role is responsible for producing and maintaining various value-add Business Intelligence reports.

Key accountabilities include, but aren't limited to:
• Define and validate data elements and data structures;
• Develop subject area models.
• Maintain data architecture artifacts for multiple systems
• Define and maintain data lineage charts for the organization.
• Work with stakeholders to elicit requirements, and develop functional and technical specifications
• Design, develop, test and maintain BI solutions, including dashboards and reports, using software life

cycle.
• Work closely with various team members on multiple concurrent projects.
• Provide input into and maintenance of the centralized reporting strategy for LABC;
• Maintain and adjust existing reports as business requirements change;

Who We Are

Legal Aid BC (LABC) is a non-profit organization established by the Legal Services Society Act in 1979. Our mission is to provide legal information, advice, and representation services to BC's most vulnerable and marginalized citizens who lack the necessary resources to effectively access the justice system when their families, freedom, or safety are at risk. Our vision is to see BC’s legal system transformed, by the way LABC represents our clients, into one more equitable and inclusive with a mission to strengthen client-centred legal help for British Columbians experiencing barriers accessing the legal system.

At LABC, we know that our continued ability to help the people who need us is due to the hardworking individuals who make up our team, dedicated to making a difference in the lives of people in need. We foster a positive culture of open communication for our team to thrive within, underpinned by our values of client focus, advancing Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, and change through leadership.

Legal Aid BC is in the early stages of, and committed to a transformative journey of embedding Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion in all aspects of our work. We seek talent from equity-seeking groups, including but not limited to individuals who self-identify as First Nations, Inuit, or Metis descent, persons with diverse abilities, gender diverse, racialized, and members of the 2SLGBTQIA+ community, be a part of this exciting stage of Legal Aid BC’s growth. We care deeply about the culture we are building and are looking for staff members who not only excel in their respective areas but are also passionate about supporting the people we serve and each other. If this resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. All interested and eligible applicants will be considered with due consideration to all protected grounds under the BC Human Rights Code. If we can do anything to make your application experience a positive and equitable one, we want to hear from you on what we can do better.

To understand why legal aid matters, and our commitments to Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, please explore our website, planning and guiding documents at https://legalaid.bc.ca/.

The Ideal Candidate
• You have bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent
• Minimum three (3) years’ related experience as outlined in rated requirements including strategic initiative project management, process and performance improvement, data analytics and reporting metrics required.
• Advanced working knowledge of reporting systems (such as SQL Server Reporting Services, Crystal Reports and/or Micro strategy)
• Advanced level in MS Excel
• Minimum 2 years’ work experience using Power BI
• Minimum of 2 years’ experience with Data warehouse and ETL tools
• Familiarity with Cloud technologies
• Familiarity with Artificial Intelligence
• Technical competencies:
• High proficiency with Microsoft Office products required.
• Excellent PowerPoint skills and design capability and high proficiency in Excel and data analysis.
• Proficiency with data visualization software, preferably Power BI as well as HTML skills preferred.
• Ability to apply data governance, data management, privacy and information security measures in various stages of the data lifecycle.
• Fluent in hybrid working environment and leveraging Zoom and Microsoft Teams to collaborate and engage with peers and develop initiatives.
• OR an equivalent combination of training, education and experience may be considered. You strive to exceed expectations and are a team player.

We Offer

A competitive annual salary, as well as a comprehensive benefits package (based on your employment status and affiliation including:
• Four weeks paid vacation to start that grows the longer you are with LABC
• An excellent employee benefits package, where premiums are 100% paid by LABC
• A generous defined benefit pension plan
• Support for training and development
• An Employee and Family Assistance program
• The opportunity to participate in various Employee programs (Employee Wellness, etc.)
• Support for a healthy work/life balance
• Generous leave provisions (sick time, special leaves)
• 13 paid statutory holidays
• An opportunity for a flexible hybrid work arrangement for some positions (LABC reserves the right to change this policy at any time)
• Transit friendly employer for eligible staff to have their commute subsidized
• Dog-friendly offices
• Dedicated Cultural Leave provisions for Indigenous employees
• An Equity, Diversity and Inclusion Council, and Indigenous employees Council and Accessibility Committee of employees from equity-seeking groups that advises on ongoing improvements and investments in Equity, Diversity and Inclusion at our workplace

For additional information regarding our extensive benefits package, please explore our careers page at: https://legalaid.bc.ca/about/careers/why-work-here.

If you are interested in joining us – visit our careers page for the full job posting at https://legalaid.bc.ca/careers and apply now!

title: Senior Data Analyst
job_description:
Are you a proactive Senior Data Analyst skilled in analytics, from data transformations to statistical analysis and machine learning? Do you thrive on solving problems and driving innovation? If you’re eager to turn insights into action and empower others, join us to shape the future of data-driven decision-making in a collaborative, forward-thinking team.

Join our dynamic, forward-thinking team as a Senior Data Analyst. In this key role, you'll work with business leaders and cross-functional teams to harness data, deliver actionable insights, and drive strategy, smarter decisions, and innovation.

We practice a remote first working model, leveraging in person interactions at our head office, in beautiful Vancouver, BC, for strategic, collaborative and social purposes.

Apply now and grow your career with Best Buy as a Senior Data Analyst.

What you’ll do:
• Be the go-to data expert for business decision support: Conduct problem research, deep-dive analysis, apply advanced analytics and ML techniques to uncover trends and insights, design high-impact reports and visuals to tell compelling data stories that inspire action and shape our offerings and operations.
• Lead the way on strategic projects: Tackle complex challenges and enable data solutions using standard and advanced analytics, automation, business intelligence, opportunity analysis, forecasting, and simulations.
• Innovate for tomorrow: Design and implement experiments that directly influence our customer experience, helping us stay ahead of the curve. Spot new analytics opportunities that propel our business forward, improve our practices to increase business outcomes and be the catalyst for positive change.

The Team
The Cumulative Effects Framework (CEF) is an established team with more than a decade of experience supporting natural resource management across British Columbia. The team is made up of three closely connected groups: the Values team, which leads assessment design; the GIS team, which conducts assessments and publishes data; and the Policy and Guidance team, which develops tools to help others interpret and apply this information. Together, they work with a wide range of professionals across the natural resource sector to produce clear, consistent assessments that support evidence‑based decisions. The CEF is a collaborative, supportive, and diverse group of experts who value innovation, shared learning, and creating high‑quality products that make a meaningful difference for communities and ecosystems in B.C.

The Role
The Marine Data Analyst supports the Cumulative Effects Framework by leading spatial and statistical analysis, developing innovative modelling approaches, and helping staff work through complex data projects specific to the marine environment. In this role, you will collaborate with subject matter experts to prepare and share high‑quality data that informs decisions across the province. This is a meaningful opportunity for someone who enjoys problem‑solving, creativity, and continuous learning, and who wants to use their analytical skills to support evidence‑based stewardship of B.C.’s lands and waters.

• Bachelor’s Degree and 2 years of related experience; OR
• An equivalent combination of education and experience may be considered.

Related Experience may include
• Experience in designing and conducting qualitative and quantitative research.

Preferences
• Experience working with large spatial datasets in a GIS software and completing spatial analysis in vector and/or raster format.
• Experience scripting spatial analyses in the Python programming language (and/or other programming language with willingness to learn Python) using common geospatial libraries.
• Ability to summarize and graphically display results from large datasets programmatically and/or in desktop software.
• Experience analyzing natural resource data relevant to the management of marine ecosystems.
• Experience assessing and analyzing natural and anthropogenic processes affecting marine ecosystems in British Columbia and/or experience working with legislation, policy and/or procedures relevant to managing the marine environment.
• Experience writing technical documents, such as guidelines, protocols, or statistical reports in a clear, concise, and effective manner for technical audiences.

title: Mining Data Analyst
job_description:
Be part of something incredible. Metals are necessary for our world, but mining historically faced issues with waste, sustainability, and inefficiencies. MineSense is changing that. Our solutions reduce mining waste and environmental impacts while increasing profits and efficiencies. Backed by the best in venture capital and government funds, and recognized by tech industry and sustainability programs, MineSense is doing what many said could not be done: improving mining for people and planet.

We are looking to add a Mining Data Analyst to our GeoScience team. The role will report to the Manager, GeoScience and be based out of the Vancouver office. The ideal candidate will have a strong interest in detailed data analysis and statistics within the mining industry. You will work with clients globally, to incorporate MineSense solutions into their ore control processes and show them the value of using our systems. If you enjoy working with data, collaborating with multidisciplinary groups, and have a mining, geology, or geophysics background, we want to hear from you!

Job Responsibilities:
• Helping new and existing clients leverage MineSense technology in their grade control engineering processes, which includes developing client facing reports and presentations.
• Collaborating with internal teams to develop prediction models by correlating mine grade measurements or estimates with our sensor results to identify patterns, trends or other data features that relate to grade, texture, mineralogy, or lithology.
• Working with the Field Service and Product Development team to further enhance our products and services through system data analysis and data quality assurance/control.
• Determining and tracking data model accuracy and precision through comparison with sampled assay grades or grade estimates.
• Reporting and presenting data results/trends to internal teams, as well as external clients.
• Working closely with the Data team to create queries, scripts, and reports while ensuring data is well organized and maintained.
• Building and maintaining good working relationships with our clients.
• Promotes a positive company culture through sharing of information and supporting the learning and development of all colleagues.
• Additional duties as occasionally required by your manager.

Qualifications:
• Degree in Earth Sciences (Geology, Geophysics), Geostatistics, Data Analytics, Mining Engineering or related field.
• Minimum of 2-4 years of relevant working experience, where experience in mining is preferred.
• Experience performing statistical analysis or data science in a work setting is preferred.
• Mining experience with in-grade control or working with ore control models is preferred.
• Experience with data analysis tools such as python or other statistical packages.
• Experience with SQL is preferred.
• Experience and passion for solving analytical problems involving diverse data sets using quantitative approaches to generate insights.
• Attention to detail and an interest in problem solving.
• Oral and written proficiency in English is required.
• Additional oral and written proficiency in Spanish is strongly preferred.
• The ability to travel and support global offices is required (10-30%).
• Legally able to work in Canada.

The salary offered to the successful candidate will be based on their experience, knowledge and skills they bring to MineSense, market data based on their location as well as internal equity. Keeping this mind, the salary offered can be outside of the range posted.

Our total rewards package offers market-researched benefits, promotes internal equity, and supports team members at all levels of our organization, and includes the following:

Our total rewards package includes:
• A competitive salary and bonus.
• Matching RRSP or 401K program (for Canadian and US employees)
• A comprehensive benefit program including an employee assistance program.
• Learning and career development programs and resources (live sessions and on-demand)
• Transit Subsidy (for roles based in Vancouver, BC)

Here's what you can expect as a part of the MineSense Team:

When you join MineSense, a trailblazing Canadian mining technology company, you become a part of our purpose to maximize global metals and minimize global impacts. We’re not just a company; we’re a Community of Innovators who forge a path toward a sustainable future. Here’s why we want you to be excited:

Purpose-Driven: At MineSense, we don’t just clock in—we live our purpose. We’re on a mission to revolutionize the mining industry, and every day, we’re one step closer to a cleaner, greener world.

Values That Resonate: Our core values—Innovation, Belonging, Resilience, and Adding Value—aren’t just buzzwords. They’re the bedrock of our culture. When you step into our team, you’re not just an employee; you’re a steward of these values.

Impactful Work: Imagine contributing to a cleaner, more sustainable planet. At MineSense, you’re shaping the future of cleantech and developing cutting-edge technology that literally hasn't been developed before. Your work matters, and it echoes beyond our walls.

People-Centric: We’re not just about profits; we’re about people. Our organization cares deeply about its employees. When you join us, you become part of a community that supports growth, well-being, and success.

Ready to make your mark? Join MineSense and be part of something bigger than yourself.

title: Staff Data Analyst - Marketing & Revenue Analytics (GTM)
job_description:
The Staff Data Analyst - Sales will lead major sales analytics initiatives, driving data-driven decisions to optimize sales strategies and improve revenue generation. You will collaborate closely with the sales team to analyze sales performance, identify trends, and provide insights that shape sales strategies. In this role, you will leverage advanced data science techniques, mentor junior analysts, and ensure the seamless integration of data insights into the broader sales operations.

This is a remote-first role and is open to applicants located within Canada and the United States where we comply with our legal hiring entities. In this role, you will report to the Senior Manager, Analytics.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

WHAT YOU’LL DO:
• Lead Major Sales Analytics Projects: Oversee and lead high-impact sales analytics projects, providing in-depth analysis of sales performance, sales forecasting, and revenue optimization to drive business decisions.
• Advanced Data Analysis & Modeling: Use predictive analytics, regression modeling, and machine learning techniques to analyze sales data, identify patterns, and forecast future sales trends.
• Sales Performance Reporting & Dashboard Creation: Develop and maintain comprehensive dashboards and reports that track key sales KPIs such as revenue growth, conversion rates, sales cycle optimization, and customer acquisition cost.
• Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with sales leadership, marketing, product teams, and finance to ensure alignment between sales strategies and business objectives, providing insights that inform key decisions and improve sales outcomes.
• Mentor Junior Analysts: Guide and mentor junior team members, helping them develop technical and analytical skills, and ensuring best practices in data analysis, reporting, and insights delivery.
• A/B Testing & Experimentation: Lead the design and execution of A/B tests and other experimentation initiatives to validate sales strategies, measure the effectiveness of campaigns, and refine sales tactics.
• Customer Segmentation & Lead Generation Optimization: Conduct in-depth analysis of customer and lead data to develop insights into customer segmentation, improving lead targeting and marketing efforts.
• Ensure Data Integrity & Best Practices: Oversee data quality checks and audits to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and integrity of sales data across systems, ensuring adherence to best practices for data management and analysis.

WHAT YOU’LL NEED:
• 5-7 years of experience in data analytics, with a focus on sales analytics or related fields.
• Strong proficiency in SQL for data extraction, manipulation, and reporting.
• Deep expertise in sales analytics platforms such as Salesforce and the related sales cycle is required
• Advanced proficiency in data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) to create actionable reports and dashboards.
• Experience with predictive modeling, forecasting, and regression analysis to support strategy development around new customer acquisition, expansion signal generation, and customer retention risk..
• Strong ability to manage stakeholders and to work with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment between sales, marketing, and product strategies.
• Proven ability to mentor junior analysts, providing technical guidance and support for their development.
• Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to present complex insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

WHO YOU ARE:
• Tenacious. You are determined to succeed, and you are motivated by the success of customers, colleagues and the community.
• Curious. You are always learning and seeking ways to make things better.
• Conscientious. You keep your promises, taking your commitments to others seriously, and you have strong integrity.
• Humble. You lead with humility and empathy, respecting and learning from the perspectives of others.

VALUES STATEMENT:

Our commitment to the principles of fundamental justice embedded in the Charter of Rights and Freedoms, and to Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion is ambitious and foundational. It informs everything we do, as we defend liberty, equality, autonomy, and dignity; as we dismantle colonization, racism, sexism, ableism, transphobia and homophobia, and all discrimination. These are our values, and they inform our hiring practices too. Our business is aiding people in severe legal distress and necessitates internal and external alignment of practicing these values. Integrating Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity, and Inclusion throughout every aspect of Legal Aid BC is critical to building that culture, and we prioritize ongoing resources to achieve that goal.

The Opportunity

As Business Intelligence Data Analyst reporting to the Senior Business Intelligence Analyst, this position is pivotal in transforming data into actionable insights through interactive and visual representations, your primary function includes defining and validating data elements and structures, developing subject area models, and maintaining data architecture across multiple systems. In addition, this role is responsible for producing and maintaining various value-add Business Intelligence reports.

Key accountabilities include, but aren't limited to:
• Define and validate data elements and data structures;
• Develop subject area models.
• Maintain data architecture artifacts for multiple systems
• Define and maintain data lineage charts for the organization.
• Work with stakeholders to elicit requirements, and develop functional and technical specifications
• Design, develop, test and maintain BI solutions, including dashboards and reports, using software life
• cycle.
• Work closely with various team members on multiple concurrent projects.
• Provide input into and maintenance of the centralized reporting strategy for LABC;
• Maintain and adjust existing reports as business requirements change;

Who We Are

Legal Aid BC (LABC) is a non-profit organization established by the Legal Services Society Act in 1979. Our mission is to provide legal information, advice, and representation services to BC's most vulnerable and marginalized citizens who lack the necessary resources to effectively access the justice system when their families, freedom, or safety are at risk. Our vision is to see BC’s legal system transformed, by the way LABC represents our clients, into one more equitable and inclusive with a mission to strengthen client-centred legal help for British Columbians experiencing barriers accessing the legal system.

At LABC, we know that our continued ability to help the people who need us is due to the hardworking individuals who make up our team, dedicated to making a difference in the lives of people in need. We foster a positive culture of open communication for our team to thrive within, underpinned by our values of client focus, advancing Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, and change through leadership.

Legal Aid BC is in the early stages of, and committed to a transformative journey of embedding Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion in all aspects of our work. We seek talent from equity-seeking groups, including but not limited to individuals who self-identify as First Nations, Inuit, or Metis descent, persons with diverse abilities, gender diverse, racialized, and members of the 2SLGBTQIA+ community, be a part of this exciting stage of Legal Aid BC’s growth. We care deeply about the culture we are building and are looking for staff members who not only excel in their respective areas but are also passionate about supporting the people we serve and each other. If this resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. All interested and eligible applicants will be considered with due consideration to all protected grounds under the BC Human Rights Code. If we can do anything to make your application experience a positive and equitable one, we want to hear from you on what we can do better.

To understand why legal aid matters, and our commitments to Truth and Reconciliation, and Equity, Diversity and Inclusion, please explore our website, planning and guiding documents at https://legalaid.bc.ca/.

The Ideal Candidate
• You have bachelor’s degree in computer science or equivalent
• Minimum three (3) years’ related experience as outlined in rated requirements including strategic initiative project management, process and performance improvement, data analytics and reporting metrics required.
• Advanced working knowledge of reporting systems (such as SQL Server Reporting Services, Crystal Reports and/or Micro strategy)
• Advanced level in MS Excel
• Minimum 2 years’ work experience using Power BI
• Minimum of 2 years’ experience with Data warehouse and ETL tools
• Familiarity with Cloud technologies
• Familiarity with Artificial Intelligence

Technical competencies:
• High proficiency with Microsoft Office products required.
• Excellent PowerPoint skills and design capability and high proficiency in Excel and data analysis.
• Proficiency with data visualization software, preferably Power BI as well as HTML skills preferred.
• Ability to apply data governance, data management, privacy and information security measures in various stages of the data lifecycle.
• Fluent in hybrid working environment and leveraging Zoom and Microsoft Teams to collaborate and engage with peers and develop initiatives.
• OR an equivalent combination of training, education and experience may be considered. You strive to exceed expectations and are a team player.

We Offer

A competitive annual salary, as well as a comprehensive benefits package (based on your employment status and affiliation including:

title: Business Intelligence Analyst Dilawri Group of Companies
job_description:
Looking for a great opportunity to work in Canada’s largest Automotive Group? Join the Family today, the Dilawri Lifestyle is waiting for you

Dilawri life is full of excitement, click here for a glimpse into the road ahead.

DilawriGroup of Companies is currently looking to fill the position of a Business Intelligence Analyst.

Under the supervision of the National Manager, Application Development and Data Intelligence, the Business Intelligence (BI) Analyst is responsible for supporting financial and operational reporting needs through hands‑on data analysis, dashboard development, and process improvement. This role requires strong technical skills in working with databases, reporting platforms, and data visualization tools to deliver accurate, timely, and actionable insights. The BI Analyst will collaborate with stakeholders across the business to gather requirements, design and maintain dashboards, and perform in‑depth analysis to identify trends, interpret results, and recommend enhancements. This is a hands‑on role focused on building, maintaining, and optimizing BI solutions to support informed decision‑making at all levels of the organization.

- Develop, consolidate, and streamline dashboards to deliver enhanced analytics and actionable insights across the organization.

- Design and build operational and financial reports and dashboards (charts, graphs, visualizations) using reporting platforms such as GCP, BigQuery, Looker (LookML), and other ERP or financial systems.

- Support implementation, maintenance, and optimization of financial databases and ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) processes.

- Assess existing data structures, identify limitations, and propose improvements or new solutions in collaboration with the National Development Team.

- Configure and maintain data interfaces to ensure complete and accurate data exchange between the company’s ERP system, data warehouse, and reporting tools.

- Partner with the national development team to help define and execute a sustainable data strategy to support organizational growth.

- Perform in‑depth data analysis and translate findings into clear, concise recommendations for business stakeholders.

- Communicate complex analytics in a simple, actionable way to non‑technical audiences through strong presentation and storytelling skills.

- Define, document, and maintain data governance practices, assumptions, and procedures to ensure reporting accuracy and reliability.

- Perform other related duties as assigned.

Qualifications

- University or post‑secondary degree in Business, Finance, Computer Science and/or related discipline along with reporting / technological accreditation.

- Minimum of 5 years experience in a financial or operational data role with proven leadership in data‑driven projects.

- Understanding of finance and accounting concepts and principles.

- Broad experience with techniques in collecting data, analyzing information, generating reports and database principles.

- Ability to design, develop and deploy financial reports with any of the reporting platforms with strong knowledge of SQL (Tableau, Looker, Power BI, Domo or Cognos etc.).

- Strong analytical skills and accounting technical knowledge.

- Ability to work in a fast‑paced high‑pressure environment.

- Reliable, self‑motivated, and self‑disciplined.

- Capability of managing multiple projects simultaneously.

- Valid Driver’s License.
