title: Revenue Operations & Business Intelligence Specialist (RevOps)
job_description:
About Jetson:

Jetson is on a mission to accelerate the transition of 100 million homes across North America away from fossil fuels toward sustainable energy use. We believe in a future that is 100% electric and 100% better.

Homes are one of the largest sources of carbon emissions, yet adoption of solutions like heat pumps remains slow due to cost and complexity. Jetson is solving this by building the first fully vertically integrated home electrification company — making clean energy simple, transparent, and affordable.

We rely heavily on technology, automation, and data to scale this impact. At Jetson, we value people who are excellent at their craft, curious about new tools (including AI), and motivated to continuously improve how work gets done.

The Opportunity:

Jetson is redefining home electrification through technology, data, and automation. We're a fast-growing, tech-forward startup building smarter systems to deliver a better customer experience at scale — AI is not a side project here, it's the primary way we build and operate.

We're looking for a Revenue Operations & Business Intelligence Specialist to own the operational and analytics backbone of our business. This is a high-impact, highly visible role sitting at the intersection of our revenue engine and company-wide data strategy. You'll own HubSpot configuration and optimization, build the reporting and dashboarding layer that powers decision-making across Sales, Marketing, Operations, Finance, and Leadership, and act as a strategic thought partner to the founding team on revenue performance and scalability.

This role is for someone who thinks like an engineer, moves like a builder, and is deeply fluent in AI tooling. You'll work with Claude Code and other AI-native tools as your primary development environment — writing code, building integrations, and shipping reporting infrastructure at a pace that wouldn't be possible otherwise. You'll work closely with our data engineering team and business leaders, driving progress independently with limited structure and a high bar for output quality.

What You Will Do:
• Revenue Operations & Systems
• Own and optimize Jetson's revenue stack, with HubSpot as the centerpiece — including CRM configuration, pipeline management, lifecycle stages, automation workflows, and integrations across Sales, Marketing, and Customer Success
• Partner with Customer Operations to ensure help desk and customer service tooling is implemented effectively as we scale
• Build and maintain integrations connecting HubSpot to NetSuite, field service tools, and other core systems — leveraging Claude Code and AI-native development workflows to move quickly
• Develop custom enablement tools and internal utilities that support revenue teams and reduce manual overhead
• Evaluate and implement new GTM tools that improve effectiveness, attribution, and visibility
• Design, implement, and document scalable end-to-end revenue processes that improve speed, consistency, and clarity across team handoffs

Business Intelligence & Reporting
• Partner closely with our data engineering team to build on top of a clean, well-structured data foundation — you own the reporting layer, they own the pipeline
• Build extensive dashboards and KPI reporting across Sales, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Leadership using AI-native toolchains — the goal is real-time operational visibility that replaces manual, spreadsheet-driven processes
• Develop employee-level performance scorecards and operational accountability systems
• Deeply understand Jetson's business processes and org structure to identify the key measurements that actually drive performance — not just surface-level metrics, but the indicators that predict outcomes
• Translate data into insights that drive decisions, not just reports that sit unused
• Identify gaps in how data is captured across the business and work with data engineering to improve instrumentation at the source

AI-Native Development
• Use Claude Code and other AI development tools as your primary workflow for building integrations, dashboards, and internal tooling — this is not optional, it's how this role operates
• Identify where AI can eliminate manual or repetitive work and unlock productivity gains across revenue functions
• Help define how Jetson responsibly and effectively operationalizes AI across GTM and operations
• Stay current with the AI tooling landscape and proactively bring new capabilities into the team's workflow

What You Bring:
• 4–6 years of experience in Revenue Operations, Business Intelligence, Software Engineering, or a closely related technical role
• Strong technical background — a software engineering foundation or equivalent hands-on build experience is highly valued
• AI-native fluency is a core requirement: you work primarily with AI coding tools (Claude Code or similar) and know how to get production-quality output from them
• Deep, hands-on HubSpot experience across Sales, Marketing, and Service Hubs — this is a must
• Strong analytical mindset with SQL proficiency and experience building dashboards and operational reporting that drive accountability
• Experience building and maintaining API integrations and custom internal tooling
• A genuine ability to understand business processes and org structure — you can sit with a business leader, understand how their team operates, and identify the 3–5 metrics that actually matter
• Strong business intuition paired with technical execution ability
• Excellent written and verbal communication skills; able to translate technical complexity into clarity for business stakeholders
• Startup or high-growth company experience; comfortable driving progress independently with limited structure

Nice to Have:
• Experience with Gong or other call intelligence platforms
• Familiarity with dbt or modern data transformation frameworks
• Experience with attribution modeling or advanced funnel analysis
• Background in field service workflows or operational analytics
• Exposure to tools like Fivetran, Airbyte, or similar ELT platforms

title: Data Science Expert - AI Content Specialist
job_description:
• Location: Remote

About The Job

At Alignerr, we partner with the world’s leading AI research teams and labs to build and train cutting-edge AI models.

You’ll challenge advanced language models on topics like machine learning theory, statistical inference, neural network architectures, and data engineering pipelines—documenting every failure mode so we can harden model reasoning.

Organization: Alignerr Position: Data Science Expert - AI Content Specialist Type: Hourly Contract Compensation: $40–$80 /hour Location: Remote Commitment: 10–40 hours/week

What You’ll Do
• Develop Complex Problems: Design advanced data science challenges across domains like hyperparameter optimization, Bayesian inference, cross-validation strategies, and dimensionality reduction.
• Author Ground-Truth Solutions: Create rigorous, step-by-step technical solutions including Python/R scripts, SQL queries, and mathematical derivations that serve as "golden responses."
• Technical Auditing: Evaluate AI-generated code (using libraries like Scikit-Learn, PyTorch, or TensorFlow), data visualizations, and statistical summaries for technical accuracy and efficiency.
• Refine Reasoning: Identify logical fallacies in AI reasoning—such as data leakage, overfitting, or improper handling of imbalanced datasets—and provide structured feedback to improve the model's "thinking" process.

Requirements
• Advanced Degree: Masters (pursuing or completed) or PhD in Data Science, Statistics, Computer Science, or a quantitative field with a heavy emphasis on data analysis.
• Domain Expertise: Strong foundational knowledge in core areas such as supervised/unsupervised learning, deep learning, big data technologies (Spark/Hadoop), or NLP.
• Analytical Writing: The ability to communicate highly technical algorithmic concepts and statistical results clearly and concisely in written form.
• Attention to Detail: High level of precision when checking code syntax, mathematical notation, and the validity of statistical conclusions.
• No AI experience required

Preferred
• Prior experience with data annotation, data quality, or evaluation systems
• Proficiency in production-level data science workflows (e.g., MLOps, CI/CD for models).

Why Join Us
• Excellent compensation with location-independent flexibility.
• Direct engagement with industry-leading LLMs.
• Contractor advantages: high agency, agility, and international reach.
• More opportunities for contracting renewals.

Application Process (Takes 15-20 min)

title: Associate Data Engineer
job_description:
Our Story & Purpose:

We’re Vancity, a member-owned credit union built on the principles of inclusion and social justice. Since 1946, our relentless commitment to these values has helped us challenge the status quo and break down barriers. We’ve made bold commitments to become net-zero by 2040 across all mortgages and loans, and we’re actively pursuing strategies in Indigenous banking and financial resilience for our members.

As the largest private sector Living Wage Employer in Canada, we’re proud to be consistently recognized as one of the country’s Top Employers. If you’re ready to join our team of 2,300 diverse individuals, access competitive rewards and benefits, and be part of a greater movement - apply today!

Your Role in Supporting Our Members:

As an Associate Data Engineer, you will play a foundational role in building and maintaining reliable, scalable data solutions that power analytics, reporting, and decision-making across the organization. You will support the development of robust data pipelines, uphold data quality standards, and contribute to a secure and well-governed data ecosystem.

Working closely with senior engineers, analysts, and business partners, you’ll help ensure our data is accurate, accessible, and actionable - enabling better outcomes for our teams and the members we serve.

This is a Full-time, Permanent role and will report directly to the Director, Data Platform. Our head office is based in Vancouver, but we are open to candidates in BC and Ontario. While this position provides a hybrid work arrangement, you will be expected to be on-site for events and business demands when needed.

How You'll Make an Impact:

Building & Maintaining Data Pipelines
• Building and maintaining ETL/ELT processes for structured and unstructured data
• Supporting the design of reliable, efficient, and scalable data pipelines across cloud and on-prem environments
• Optimizing workflows to improve performance and reduce processing time

Production Support & Issue Resolution
• Monitoring data pipelines and systems for errors, failures, or performance issues
• Troubleshooting and resolving data-related incidents in a timely manner to minimize operational disruption
• Supporting root cause analysis and implementing preventative improvements

Data Quality & Governance
• Validating data accuracy, completeness, and integrity across systems
• Applying data governance best practices, including naming conventions, access controls, and compliance standards
• Supporting audits and ensuring alignment with regulatory and security requirements

Documentation & Knowledge Sharing
• Creating and maintaining clear technical documentation for data workflows, architecture, and processes
• Ensuring documentation supports long-term maintainability and effective team knowledge transfer

Collaboration & Communication
• Partnering closely with senior data engineers, data analysts, and business stakeholders to understand requirements and deliver solutions
• Communicating technical concepts, progress updates, and challenges clearly and effectively
• Contributing positively within an agile, cross-functional team environment

Continuous Learning & Improvement
• Staying current with emerging data engineering tools, technologies, and industry best practices
• Contributing ideas for automation, optimization, and process improvement
• Actively participating in training and skill development opportunities

What You’ll Bring to the Team:
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Data Engineering, Information Systems, Software Engineering, or a related field (required)
• 1-3 years of professional experience in data engineering, data integration, or a related technical field
• Hands-on experience developing and maintaining ETL/ELT processes and batch data pipelines (streaming exposure is a plus)
• Exposure to data modeling concepts and supporting analytics use cases.
• Experience working within cloud environments such as Azure, AWS, or GCP.
• Familiarity with BI or data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau) is an asset.
• Strong proficiency in SQL and working knowledge of at least one programming language (e.g., Python).
• Foundational understanding of data architecture and modeling concepts (e.g., star schema, snowflake schema, basic lakehouse patterns).
• Familiarity with modern data platforms and tools such as:
• Big data & compute: Spark, Databricks, Snowflake (basic exposure preferred)
• Orchestration: Airflow, Azure Data Factory, or dbt
• Version control & DevOps: Git, Azure DevOps, with awareness of CI/CD principles
• Understanding of data governance, data quality, and data security best practices.
• Ongoing learning in data engineering tools, SQL, and scripting languages is encouraged.

You’ll Thrive Here If You Are:
• Strategic and risk-aware - You connect capital, liquidity, and balance sheet decisions to enterprise risk appetite and anticipate emerging vulnerabilities.
• Analytical - You apply rigor to financial modeling and stress testing, challenge assumptions thoughtfully, and deliver clear, defensible insights.
• Collaborative - You build trusted cross-functional relationships and provide independent and objective insights that strengthen decision-making.

We value lived experience, so if you are interested in this role, we encourage you to apply even if you feel your skills don't perfectly align with those listed.

What You’ll Earn:

This role offers a salary range of $83,300 to $110,000 per annum. The base pay offered may vary depending on factors such as relevant qualifications, skills, previous experience, and internal equity. As part of our total rewards package, employees may also be eligible for our annual incentive program, subject to program eligibility requirements.

Why You’ll Love Working Here:

A career at Vancity is more than just a job, you’re joining a tradition of change-makers who are creating lasting change for our communities. Beyond base pay, we offer a comprehensive total rewards package to ensure our employees are empowered to thrive:
• Living Wage Employer: We’re the largest private-sector Living Wage Employer in Canada and consistently ranked among Canada’s Top Employers.
• Customizable Benefits: Permanent employees receive flexible benefit packages that can be tailored annually to meet evolving needs.
• Generous Vacation: New employees start with 3-4 weeks of vacation per year, with additional days earned over time.
• Extra Stat Holidays: In addition to BC’s 11 statutory holidays, we offer 2 extra days, plus care days for personal or family illness.
• Immediate Health Coverage: Health and dental benefits begin on your hire date, with three levels of coverage to choose from.
• Defined Benefit Pension: Our retirement plan provides a guaranteed income for life, recognizing that retirement looks different for everyone.

Vancity Talent Programs:

Vancity supports an inclusive hiring process for candidates who self-identify as Indigenous, Black, or Trans. With special permission from the BC Human Rights Commissioner, this initiative provides access to career development opportunities, prioritized job screening, and feedback. Any information you choose to share will be stored securely and used only for recruitment and career development connected to this initiative, in line with the BC Personal Information Protection Act (PIPA). For details, please see our dedicated Talent Programs job posting.

We are also committed to an inclusive, barrier-free and accessible recruitment experience for all candidates. If you require any accommodations or support at any stage of the recruitment process (including the application stage), we encourage you to let us know by contacting our Talent Acquisition team at recruitment@vancity.com. We’re here to work with you to ensure your needs are met promptly and effectively. All requests will be handled with the utmost respect and confidentiality, so you can participate fully in the process.

title: Software Engineer, Data Infrastructure & Acquisition - Vancouver, Canada
job_description:
The mission of Speechify is to make sure that reading is never a barrier to learning.

Over 50 million people use Speechify's text-to-speech products to turn whatever they're reading - PDFs, books, Google Docs, news articles, websites - into audio, so they can read faster, read more, and remember more. Speechify's text-to-speech reading products include its iOS app, Android App, Mac App, Chrome Extension, and Web App. Google recently named Speechify the Chrome Extension of the Year and Apple named Speechify its 2025 Design Award winner for Inclusivity.

Today, nearly 200 people around the globe work on Speechify in a 100% distributed setting - Speechify has no office. These include frontend and backend engineers, AI research scientists, and others from Amazon, Microsoft, and Google, leading PhD programs like Stanford, high growth startups like Stripe, Vercel, Bolt, and many founders of their own companies.

Overview

We're looking to hire for our Data side of our AI team at Speechify. This role is responsible for all aspects of data collection to support our model training operations. We are able to build high-quality datasets at petabyte-scale and low cost through a tight integration of infrastructure, engineering, and research work. We are looking for a skilled Software Engineer to join us.

What You'll Do
• Be scrappy to find new sources of audio data and bring it into our ingestion pipeline
• Operate and extend the cloud infrastructure for our ingestion pipeline, currently running on GCP and managed with Terraform.
• Collaborate closely with our Scientists to shift the cost/throughput/quality frontier, delivering richer data at bigger scale and lower cost to power our next-generation models.
• Collaborate with others on the AI Team and Speechify Leadership to craft the AI Team's dataset roadmap to power Speechify's next-generation consumer and enterprise products.

An Ideal Candidate Should Have
• BS/MS/PhD in Computer Science or a related field.
• 5+ years of industry experience in software development.
• Proficiency with bash/Python scripting in Linux environments
• Proficiency in Docker and Infrastructure-as-Code concepts and professional experience with at least one major Cloud Provider (we use GCP)
• Experience with web crawlers, large-scale data processing workflows is a plus
• Ability to handle multiple tasks and adapt to changing priorities.
• Strong communication skills, both written and verbal.

What we offer
• A fast-growing environment where you can help shape the company and product.
• An entrepreneurial-minded team that supports risk, intuition, and hustle.
• A hands-off management approach so you can focus and do your best work.
• An opportunity to make a big impact in a transformative industry.
• Competitive salaries, a friendly and laid-back atmosphere, and a commitment to building a great asynchronous culture.
• Opportunity to work on a life-changing product that millions of people use.
• Build products that directly impact and support people with learning differences like dyslexia, ADD, low vision, concussions, autism, and more.
• Work in one of the fastest-growing sectors of tech, the intersection of artificial intelligence and audio.

Think you're a good fit for this job?

Tell us more about yourself and why you're interested in the role when you apply.
And don't forget to include links to your portfolio and LinkedIn.

Not looking but know someone who would make a great fit?

Refer them!

Speechify does not discriminate on the basis of race, national origin, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, or other legally protected status.

As a primary contributor to the evolution of Marketing measurement, this role will help drive innovation, foster a data-driven culture, and collaborate cross-functionally to guide ongoing optimization efforts.

title: Analyst, Business Intelligence
job_description:
Vancouver Whitecaps FC is looking for a new team member to join our Business Intelligence department in the position of **Analyst, Business Intelligence & CRM.**

The Analyst, Business Intelligence & CRM will provide analytical support to drive business decisions while simultaneously overseeing the effective use and optimization of our Salesforce Sales Cloud platform. This role bridges data analytics and CRM support to enhance customer engagement, operational efficiency, and data-driven insights. Strong communication, collaboration, and project management skills are essential for success in this dual role.
• *Key Responsibilities**:

- **Business Analytics**: Leverage data to inform strategic decisions, using business intelligence tools to identify and communicate key trends, patterns, and actionable insights.
- **Salesforce Support**: Lead the administration, optimization, and maintenance of the Salesforce Sales Cloud platform, ensuring system stability and usability.
- **Campaign Effectiveness Tracking**:Oversee tracking and analytics for sales campaigns from creation through execution and evaluate performance to optimize outcomes.
- **Cross-Department Collaboration**: Collaborate with various departments to gather requirements and deliver CRM and analytics solutions that align with business needs.
- **Workflow Development**: Create and manage CRM workflows, such as automated leads distribution, to streamline operations, boost fan engagement, and support decision-making processes.
- **Project Management**: Lead cross-functional projects, managing timelines and resources to support the Business Intelligence & CRM strategy in alignment with the Club’s objectives.
- **Data Quality & Governance**: Ensure data quality and hygiene across platforms, managing user roles, permissions, and security settings to maintain a secure CRM environment.
- **Ad-hoc Analysis & Data Collection**: Support data collection efforts, including fan surveys, and provide ad-hoc analysis as needed.
- **Training & Support**: Provide training and ongoing support for Salesforce users, empowering them to maximize platform functionality.
- **Internal & External Representation**:Act as a representative of the Business Intelligence team in both internal and external engagements, fostering collaboration and partnership.
• *Qualifications/Experience**:

- Legally authorized to work in Canada.
- 3+ years of experience providing data analytics or support, preferably in a corporate setting.
- Problem-solving mindset with a focus on continuous improvement and innovation.
- Proficiency in Tableau or other data visualization tools.
- Strong project management skills with experience leading cross-functional initiatives.
- Experience with sales or marketing campaign reporting & analytics a bonus.
- Salesforce CRM Administrator certification or a willingness to obtain it.
- Familiarity with Salesforce custom objects, fields, workflows nice to have.
- Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to collaborate with stakeholders at all levels.
- Adaptability and flexibility in a fast-paced environment, with the ability to prioritize tasks effectively.
• *The Club**

Vancouver Whitecaps FC is the longest-running professional soccer club in Canada and the United States. Since its inaugural season in 1974, the club has united generations of loyal fans in British Columbia and beyond. With academies in eight out of ten provinces, a network of partner clubs, and hundreds of camps and clinics every year, Whitecaps FC provide a foundation for Canada’s soccer future.

We are also strongly committed to providing opportunities to Canadian athletes, both male and female, with the largest soccer development structure in Canada. This includes academy programs in eight of ten provinces across the country.

In addition to building a winning team on and off the pitch, the Club places great importance in giving back to our community, harnessing the power of sport to create a stronger, more equitable & inclusive society through three key community goals: helping meet critical needs, using soccer to build life skills and creating soccer participation opportunities in Indigenous communities.

We thank all applicants for their interest, however, only those selected for further consideration will be contacted.

All we ask is that you be ethical and inclusive in all that you do. Be respectful of all players, fans, and staff, and remember to cheer loudest for the ones wearing the blue and white. Go ‘Caps"_

title: REMOTE - Data Science Analyst
job_description:
Data Analyst (Hybrid) Job Number:

Bachelor's Degree JOB: Project and Program Management EMPLOYMENT TYPE: Full Time JOB LEVEL: Flexible working arrangements

ETS provides capabilities for all stages of the Firm's software development lifecycle, enabling productive coding, functional and integration testing, application releases, and ongoing monitoring and support for over 3,000 production applications. ETS also delivers all workplace technologies (desktop, mobile, voice, video, productivity, intranet/internet) in integrated configurations that boost the personal productivity of our employees. Application and end user services are delivered on a scalable, secure, and reliable infrastructure composed of seamlessly integrated datacenter, network, compute, cloud, storage, and database services. ETS' mission is to ensure the Firm manages its global businesses and serve clients on a marketing-leading technology platform that is resilient, safe, smart, fast, flexible, and efficient. The Enterprise Computing Data & Analytics Technology (DAT) department sits within ETS and enables Data and Analytics Technologies to support our businesses in data processing, creating new insights and value via analytics. Focusing on a) providing modern data & analytics technologies enabled for Modern On-Prem & Public Cloud (b) sustaining the on-premises data & analytics plant in accordance with customer needs, security, risk & stability requirements (c) delivering target capabilities for data & analytics technologies in support of the Firm's Policies. We built and maintained a robust key performance indicator (KPI) framework to provide consistent and standard measurements to identify gaps, recognize achievements and propose enhancements - constantly seeking opportunities to improve our reporting, methods of measurement and data quality. DAT Analytics builds diverse report and dashboard solutions to increase organizational transparency and encourage observability/DevOps best practices. Our team generates critical reports that ensure we meet regulatory requirements - encryption, immutable backups. You will engage in the entire data life cycle - collect requirements, identify or build data source, analyze data, build data pipelines to load and transform data, visualize and present data.

Ensure data quality, manage, and scale data solutions.

Implementing appropriate industry-standard practices, including defining and reporting on key metrics, identifying risks, issues, gaps, and dependencies within and across work streams.

Assistance with documenting data models and flows, artifacts, and responsibilities.

Develop, analyze, and present findings in response to senior management requests.

Demonstrate good time management and ability to prioritize tasks and to meet deadlines across multiple projects.

Must be flexible, highly adaptable to a constantly changing environment. The ideal candidate will be a self-motivated and flexible team player who has a strategic vision but also the ability to execute successfully.

3+ years of experience working with data analytics / visualization software.
• Proficiency in Python and SQL.
• Bachelor's degree in Computer Science or a related field
• Ability to analyze / synthesize large amounts of information and present findings to senior audiences.
• Ability to be flexible with ever-changing prioritization.
• Creative thinker who will take complete ownership and accountability.

Background in statistics or data science is a plus.

Power BI, Tableau or Splunk preferred.

About us: Morgan Stanley is a global financial services firm and a market leader in investment banking, securities, investment management and wealth management services. At Morgan Stanley Montreal, we are shaping the future of our global business and contributing to our local community. Our skilled and creative workforce is comprised of individuals drawn from a broad cross section of the global communities in which we operate and who reflect a variety of backgrounds, talents, perspectives, and experiences. Our strong commitment to a culture of inclusion is evident through our constant focus on recruiting, developing, and advancing individuals based on their skills and talents. We offer a hybrid work environment, combining remote work and attendance in the office. Knowledge of French and English is required.

title: Summer Student – Data Analyst, Learning and Development
job_description:
For 75 years, Charles River employees have worked together to assist in the discovery, development and safe manufacture of new drug therapies. When you join our family, you will have a significant impact on the health and well-being of people across the globe. Whether your background is in life sciences, finance, IT, sales or another area, your skills will play an important role in the work we perform. In return, we’ll help you build a career that you can feel passionate about.

Job Summary

As a
Summer Student, Data Analyst
for our Global
Learning & Development
team, you will collaborate with the Global Safety Assessment Team by assisting with the digitalization of harmonized training records in a Learning Management System (LMS). This position is responsible for assisting in the coordination of project activities, maintenance and updating of the project schedules, ensuring data accuracy, managing assignments, monitoring progress, and communicating to the project team.

In this role, primary
responsibilities
include:
• Review selected data for accuracy against the provided reference values;
• Flag inconsistencies and work with the team to correct the quality/completeness of the data;
• Utilize standard spreadsheets to capture required details of assigned reviews;
• Prepare summary of observations;
• Work with the team to remediate findings;
• Provide regular updates on progress and communicate any impediment;
• Assisting the project manager with administrative duties and processes;
• Reporting on team and project performance;
• Obtaining and performing data analytics across multiple data sources;
• Creation and maintenance of project management tools, reports, and SharePoint sites;
• In conjunction with users and managers, defining requirements/user stories, documenting specifications, implementing and supporting operational information systems;
• Scheduling meetings and keeping minutes.

Key Elements

We are looking for the following
minimum qualifications
for this role:
• Currently enrolled in an accredited college/university degree program in biological sciences, business management or related discipline.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Office and Excel is required.
• Must be willing to be trained as an LMS system administrator.
• Demonstrate excellent written and verbal communication skills, organizational skills, and attention to details.
• Ability to learn new technologies quickly and ability to work independently and be proactive is required.

Role Specific Information:
• Location
: Remote;
• Schedule
: Monday-Friday, day schedule;
• Temporary position, full-time 37.5hrs per week.

Why Charles River ?
• Employee and family assistance program;
• Excellent welcome program for new employees;
• Many social activities!

If you are interested in contributing to the well-being of our communities, not only across the country but around the world, please join us.
This is Your Moment.

About Safety Assessment

Charles River is committed to helping our partners expedite their preclinical drug development with exceptional safety assessment services, state-of-the-art facilities and expert regulatory guidance. From individual specialty toxicology and IND enabling studies to tailored packages and total laboratory support, our deeply experienced team can design and execute programs that anticipate challenges and avoid roadblocks for a smooth, efficient journey to market. Each year approximately 300 investigational new drug (IND) programs are conducted in our Safety Assessment facilities.

About Charles River

Charles River is an early-stage contract research organization (CRO). We have built upon our foundation of laboratory animal medicine and science to develop a diverse portfolio of discovery and safety assessment services, both Good Laboratory Practice (GLP) and non-GLP, to support clients from target identification through preclinical development. Charles River also provides a suite of products and services to support our clients’ clinical laboratory testing needs and manufacturing activities. Utilizing this broad portfolio of products and services enables our clients to create a more flexible drug development model, which reduces their costs, enhances their productivity and effectiveness to increase speed to market.

With over 20,000 employees within 110 facilities in over 20 countries around the globe, we are strategically positioned to coordinate worldwide resources and apply multidisciplinary perspectives in resolving our client’s unique challenges. Our client base includes global pharmaceutical companies, biotechnology companies, government agencies and hospitals and academic institutions around the world.

At Charles River, we are passionate about our role in improving the quality of people’s lives. Our mission, our excellent science and our strong sense of purpose guide us in all that we do, and we approach each day with the knowledge that our work helps to improve the health and well-being of many across the globe. We have proudly worked on 80% of the drugs approved by the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA) in the past five years.

We’re committed to providing benefits that elevate your quality of life. Based on your position these may include: bonus/incentives based on performance, 401K, paid time off, stock purchase program, Health and wellness coverage, employee and family wellbeing support programs, and work life balance flexibility.

title: Manager, Data Integration & Business Intelligence Center of Excellence; CoE
job_description:
Position: Manager, Data Integration & Business Intelligence Center of Excellence (CoE)
Location: Dorval

When applicable, Bombardier promotes flexible and hybrid work policies.

Why join us?

At Bombardier, we design, build and maintain the world’s peak-performing aircraft for the world’s most discerning people and businesses, governments and militaries. We have been successful in setting the highest standards by putting our people at the heart of it all, and defining excellence, together.

Working at Bombardier means operating at the highest level. Every day, you are part of a team that delivers superior experiences and products, pushing the boundaries of what's possible in our industry and beyond. By prioritizing employee growth and development, we empower everyone to reach their full potential on their own terms, because the best work happens when you are free to be yourself and share your unique expertise.

Bombardier’s Benefits Program

With our employees’ well-being top of mind, we offer a comprehensive and competitive Benefits Program, which includes the following:
• Insurance plans (Dental, medical, life insurance, disability, and more)
• Competitive base salary
• Retirement savings plan
• Employee Assistance Program
• Tele Health Program

What are your contributions to the team?
• Define and execute a multi-year strategy and roadmap for the Data Integration & BI CoE, aligned with organizational objectives and industry leading practices.
• Build and lead a high-performing team of data pipeline developers, data modelers, BI developers, and platform specialists, fostering innovation, collaboration and continuous learning.
• Set clear performance objectives for the team, track team progress, and implement development plans to maintain high engagement and achievement.
• Oversee the build and operational support of the enterprise data platform (Lakehouse, data warehouse), ensuring scalability, security, and high availability.
• Lead the design, development, and maintenance of ELT/ETL pipelines, data flows, and reporting solutions using Azure Data Factory, Databricks, and Power BI.
• Ensure technology selection, deployment, and lifecycle management for all data integration and BI tools.
• Enforce data management in accordance with security, privacy and data governance directives, to ensure data quality, integrity, and regulatory compliance.
• Enable the use of self-service analytics platforms and empower business analysts through training, documentation, and support.
• Cultivate a community of practice for BI and data integration, promoting knowledge sharing and peer learning.
• Develop and oversee a cost-effective support model for data solutions, balancing internal and external resources to meet SLAs.
• Define, track, and report on key performance indicators (KPIs), ROI, and the impact of data initiatives.
• Manage budgets, resource allocation, and vendor relationships as needed.
• Partner with business leaders to identify high-impact opportunities for data integration and analytics.
• Communicate complex technical concepts and insights to non-technical stakeholders, supporting data-driven decision-making.

How to thrive in this role?
• You hold a bachelor’s or master’s degree in computer science, Engineering, Data Science, or a related field.
• You have 8+ years of experience in data integration, analytics, or data platform management, with at least 3 years in a leadership or CoE management role.
• You have proven expertise in Lakehouse architecture, Azure Data Factory, Databricks, and Power BI.
• You have a strong understanding of data modeling, data warehousing, and cloud data platforms.
• You have experience with software development, with a strong understanding of Dev Ops practices, data governance, and regulatory compliance.
• You have the demonstrated ability to lead technical teams and manage complex, cross-functional projects.
• You have proven experience in managing both project deliveries and operational support.
• You are skilled in creating impactful presentations with PowerPoint. 
• You possess excellent communication, stakeholder management, and change-leadership skills.
• You demonstrate strong verbal and written communication skills in both French and English within a professional setting
• Certifications in Azure, Databricks, Power BI, or project management are considered assets.

Now that you can see yourself in this role, apply and join the Bombardier Team!

Please note:

You don’t need all the skills, knowledge, and experience listed to apply for this position. We’re not looking for the perfect candidate, we’re looking for great talent and passionate individuals.

Bombardier is an equal opportunity employer and encourages persons of any race, religion, ethnicity, gender identity, sexual orientation, age, immigration status, disability or other applicable legally protected characteristics to apply.

title: Business Intelligence Engineer
job_description:
Position: Senior Engineer - Business Intelligence

Work Location: Canada (Remote)

Type of Employment: Full-Time

Purpose of the Position: Lead the migration from Power BI to Strategy (MicroStrategy), define the enterprise semantic layer on BigQuery, and establish governance for RLS, subscriptions, and dashboard rationalization/redesign.

Key Result Areas and Activities:
• BI Strategy & Standards: Define enterprise reporting strategy on Strategy (MicroStrategy), including modelling conventions, object governance, performance SLAs, and security (RLS).
• Migration Leadership: Inventory existing Power BI assets; rationalize/redesign dashboards; rebuild semantic models on BigQuery; establish subscriptions and distribution.
• Cross‑Platform Integration: Ensure seamless connectivity with BigQuery/Databricks outputs and alignment with medallion/serving patterns.
• Enablement & QA: Provide standards to delivery teams; support UAT, hypercare, and comprehensive documentation of BI assets.
• Design, build, and optimize dossiers/reports, prompts/filters/selectors, metrics, and reusable objects; tune performance (caching/aggregations, VLDB settings) for BigQuery sources.
• Configure subscriptions/distributions and RLS implementations; perform iterative performance troubleshooting and usage analysis to meet SLAs.

Essential Skills:
• Expert‑level MicroStrategy/Strategy architecture (semantic layer, RLS, caching/distribution) with BigQuery backends; proven experience leading Power BI to Strategy migrations.
• Hands-on development capability to independently deliver/maintain dossiers, documents, grids/graphs, metrics/filters/prompts, and optimize query performance.

Desirable Skills:
• Exposure to Looker Studio where appropriate in the target landscape.
• Understanding of pipeline implications on semantic performance (Databricks/Composer integrations).
• Retail domain knowledge is a plus.
• Experience of working with Agile.

Qualifications:
• BE Degree in Computer Science, Computer Engineering, MIS or other technical disciplines.
• MicroStrategy certifications preferred.

Qualities:
• Proven ability to develop successful relationships with external and internal partners.
• Excellent oral and written communication skills.
• Demonstrated problem solving, multi-tasking, troubleshooting skills with a high degree of flexibility.
• Demonstrated self-leadership with the ability to work independently, lead team and manage conflict.
• Proven learning agility as data technologies and capabilities evolve rapidly.
• Should be able to provide technical support to other team members as required.

title: Analyste Finance & Business Intelligence - Montréal (Hybride)
job_description:
Une opportunité stimulante s’adresse à un Analyste Finance & Business Intelligence passionné par l’analyse de données et la performance financière. Ce mandat offre l’occasion de jouer un rôle clé dans l’exploitation et la valorisation des données afin de soutenir la prise de décision stratégique et améliorer la visibilité sur les indicateurs financiers et opérationnels.

Dans ce rôle, vous serez au cœur de la transformation des données en analyses pertinentes, tableaux de bord dynamiques et solutions de reporting automatisées. Vous contribuerez activement à optimiser les processus analytiques et à fournir aux équipes de gestion des informations claires, fiables et exploitables.

Ce mandat de 6 mois, avec possibilité de renouvellement, s’adresse à un professionnel analytique, rigoureux et orienté solutions, souhaitant évoluer dans un environnement dynamique où finance, technologie et intelligence d’affaires convergent pour créer de la valeur.

AVANTAGES:
• Mandat temporaire de 6 mois avec possibilité de renouvellement, offrant une opportunité d’impact rapide et concret.
• Taux horaire flexible selon l’expérience et l’expertise du candidat.
• Rôle stratégique permettant de contribuer directement aux décisions d’affaires grâce à l’analyse financière et aux outils de Business Intelligence.
• Opportunité de travailler avec des outils analytiques modernes, notamment Power BI et Power Query.
• Mandat offrant une forte exposition aux données financières et opérationnelles, permettant de développer une vision globale de la performance.
• Possibilité de développer et d’optimiser des solutions analytiques et de reporting, contribuant à l’amélioration des processus.

RESPONSABILITÉS:
• Réaliser des analyses financières approfondies afin d’identifier les tendances, les écarts de performance et les opportunités d’amélioration, tout en soutenant la prise de décision stratégique.
• Concevoir, développer et maintenir des tableaux de bord interactifs et des rapports analytiques dans Power BI afin de fournir une visualisation claire et efficace des indicateurs clés de performance (KPI).
• Utiliser Power Query pour extraire, transformer et structurer les données provenant de multiples sources afin d’assurer leur qualité, leur cohérence et leur fiabilité pour les analyses financières.
• Effectuer des analyses de variance et des suivis budgétaires (budget, prévisions, résultats réels) afin de fournir des analyses financières pertinentes aux équipes de gestion.
• Analyser et modéliser les données financières et opérationnelles afin de générer des recommandations stratégiques et soutenir les initiatives d’amélioration de la performance.
• Collaborer étroitement avec les équipes finance, TI et opérationnelles afin de comprendre les besoins analytiques et développer des solutions de Business Intelligence adaptées.
• Optimiser et automatiser les processus de collecte, de transformation et de reporting des données afin d’améliorer l’efficacité, la rapidité et la fiabilité des analyses financières.

QUALIFICATIONS:
• Baccalauréat en finance, comptabilité, administration des affaires, analytique ou dans un domaine connexe.
• Expérience pertinente en analyse financière, Business Intelligence ou analyse de données.
• Excellente maîtrise de Power BI, incluant la création de tableaux de bord et la modélisation de données.
• Bonne connaissance de Power Query et des processus de transformation de données.
• Solides compétences analytiques et capacité à interpréter et synthétiser des données complexes.
• Maîtrise avancée d’Excel et des outils de reporting financier.
• Capacité à communiquer clairement les résultats d’analyses et à collaborer avec différentes équipes d’affaires.

EN BREF:

Contactez-moi si ce poste vous intéresse ou si vous souhaitez discuter d'opportunités de carrière en finance/comptabilité.

title: Data Scientist/ Applied Data Analyst
job_description:
Why Cellar Insights

Founded in 2023, Cellar Insights provides critical insights into perishable-crop storage—starting with potatoes. Our smart storage monitoring and early spoilage detection help growers and processors reduce their risk due to spoilage, shrink and color, and optimize movement timing across multi-site operations. Deployed across North America, the platform analyzes 1.5M+ storage datasets and monitors 300M+ lb of potatoes. Web and mobile dashboards deliver timely alerts and trend analytics; advanced analytics and AI refine predictions behind the scenes—so teams act earlier, waste less, and increase crop value.

Role Overview

We’re hiring a Data Scientist / Applied Data Analyst to become part of the analytical backbone of our product team.

This role is centered on daily interrogation of data — exploring patterns, questioning results, validating behavior in real-world conditions, and pressure-testing assumptions before they turn into product decisions. You’ll work at the intersection of Product and Engineering, helping us turn complex environmental and system data into insights that meaningfully shape what we build next.

Our data and engineering foundations are in place, and our ML platform is operating in production. What we’re looking for now is someone who can unlock the potential inside that data — exploring it deeply, challenging interpretations, and helping the team understand what the data is truly telling us.

You’ll report into Product and collaborate closely with Engineering and domain experts. This is a role for someone who wants their analysis to directly influence product direction and real-world outcomes.

What you will work on

Your work will span a range of analytical questions that support product learning and decision-making, including:
• Validation and refinement: Helping validate logic and assumptions by connecting product behavior to real-world outcomes and testing ideas before they reach production.
• Sensor and signal understanding: Exploring and characterizing deployed sensor signals across conditions to better understand behavior, limitations, and meaningful patterns.
• System comparison and benchmarking: Analyzing and comparing internal data with integrated systems to understand differences in behavior, reliability, and data quality.
• ML and event support: Conduct structured analysis and event identification that strengthens validation, supervised learning, and downstream model improvements.
• Future-ready data thinking: Helping define data needs and structures that support upcoming product initiatives and reduce future constraints.
• Exploratory investigation: Conducting hypothesis-driven exploration to challenge assumptions, uncover insights, and identify opportunities that inform product direction.

Technical Environment

You'll be consuming data from a GraphQL API and working with DynamoDB and flat file data sources. Our ML platform is in production; the analytics layer that connects the business and product team to that data is where you'll focus your energy and creativity.

What you bring

3-5 years of hands-on experience in a data science, applied analytics, or quantitative research role — ideally within a product or engineering environment where your work directly influenced decisions.
• Python — Your primary working language for analysis, modeling, and data manipulation
• SQL — Comfortable writing queries against production data stores
• Time-series and sensor data — Experience working with high-frequency, real-world operational data (imperfect, noisy, and interesting)
• Statistical methods — Solid grounding in hypothesis testing, regression, distribution analysis, and experimental design
• Predictive analytics — Experience building and validating predictive models, not just descriptive dashboards
• Visualization and communication — Ability to build and use tools like Grafana to explore data and communicate findings clearly to non-technical stakeholders

Working Style
• You question assumptions reflexively — if a number looks right, you check it anyway
• You're comfortable with ambiguity and evolving problem statements
• You balance analytical rigor with speed and pragmatism
• You build understanding before you build slides
• You thrive in small teams where your work is visible and consequential

Nice to Have
• Experience in AgTech, agriculture, food production, or IoT/sensor-based systems
• Familiarity with AWS services (DynamoDB, S3, Lambda)
• Background in applied ML or feature engineering for production models
• Experience with Grafana or similar operational analytics tools

Why this role is for you
• You'll have real impact, fast. In a small, focused team, your analysis won't sit in a slide deck — it will change what we build, how we deliver our products, and how we protect millions of dollars of stored crop.
• The data is genuinely fascinating, and you are critical to connecting the dots. You'll work with environmental sensor data, crop storage risk signals, multi-season agricultural patterns, and cross-system integrations. This is real-world data our users make decisions from — the kind of work that makes data science rewarding.
• You're joining at the right time. We have a working product, paying customers, and major processor partnerships forming. The analytics capability you build will directly support our path to building on our foundations and shaping the product for years.
• The mission matters. Potato storage losses represent massive food waste and economic harm to family farming operations. The work you do here protects livelihoods, reduces food waste, and proves that precision agriculture works beyond the field.

How to Apply?

Send your application to apply@cellarinsights.com
• Include the role you're applying for in the subject line
• Provide a cover letter detailing your relevant experience and why you are interested in joining Cellar Insights Inc.

title: Staff Data Analyst - Marketing & Revenue Analytics (GTM)
job_description:
The Staff Data Analyst - Sales will lead major sales analytics initiatives, driving data-driven decisions to optimize sales strategies and improve revenue generation. You will collaborate closely with the sales team to analyze sales performance, identify trends, and provide insights that shape sales strategies. In this role, you will leverage advanced data science techniques, mentor junior analysts, and ensure the seamless integration of data insights into the broader sales operations.

This is a remote-first role and is open to applicants located within Canada and the United States where we comply with our legal hiring entities. In this role, you will report to the Senior Manager, Analytics.

________________________________________________________________________________________________________

WHAT YOU’LL DO:
• Lead Major Sales Analytics Projects: Oversee and lead high-impact sales analytics projects, providing in-depth analysis of sales performance, sales forecasting, and revenue optimization to drive business decisions.
• Advanced Data Analysis & Modeling: Use predictive analytics, regression modeling, and machine learning techniques to analyze sales data, identify patterns, and forecast future sales trends.
• Sales Performance Reporting & Dashboard Creation: Develop and maintain comprehensive dashboards and reports that track key sales KPIs such as revenue growth, conversion rates, sales cycle optimization, and customer acquisition cost.
• Cross-Functional Collaboration: Work closely with sales leadership, marketing, product teams, and finance to ensure alignment between sales strategies and business objectives, providing insights that inform key decisions and improve sales outcomes.
• Mentor Junior Analysts: Guide and mentor junior team members, helping them develop technical and analytical skills, and ensuring best practices in data analysis, reporting, and insights delivery.
• A/B Testing & Experimentation: Lead the design and execution of A/B tests and other experimentation initiatives to validate sales strategies, measure the effectiveness of campaigns, and refine sales tactics.
• Customer Segmentation & Lead Generation Optimization: Conduct in-depth analysis of customer and lead data to develop insights into customer segmentation, improving lead targeting and marketing efforts.
• Ensure Data Integrity & Best Practices: Oversee data quality checks and audits to ensure the accuracy, consistency, and integrity of sales data across systems, ensuring adherence to best practices for data management and analysis.

WHAT YOU’LL NEED:
• 5-7 years of experience in data analytics, with a focus on sales analytics or related fields.
• Strong proficiency in SQL for data extraction, manipulation, and reporting.
• Deep expertise in sales analytics platforms such as Salesforce and the related sales cycle is required
• Advanced proficiency in data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI) to create actionable reports and dashboards.
• Experience with predictive modeling, forecasting, and regression analysis to support strategy development around new customer acquisition, expansion signal generation, and customer retention risk..
• Strong ability to manage stakeholders and to work with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment between sales, marketing, and product strategies.
• Proven ability to mentor junior analysts, providing technical guidance and support for their development.
• Exceptional communication skills, with the ability to present complex insights to both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

WHO YOU ARE:
• Tenacious. You are determined to succeed, and you are motivated by the success of customers, colleagues and the community.
• Curious. You are always learning and seeking ways to make things better.
• Conscientious. You keep your promises, taking your commitments to others seriously, and you have strong integrity.
• Humble. You lead with humility and empathy, respecting and learning from the perspectives of others.
